questions.explanation: Correct: A. Miller (1953) used CH₄ + NH₃ + H₂O + H₂ with electric discharge → amino acids.
questions.ncert_line: Miller used CH4, NH3, H2O and H2 (simulating early earth) with electric discharge to produce amino acids.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Natural selection acts on:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Natural selection acts on phenotype. The genotype is exposed to selection only through phenotype.
questions.ncert_line: Natural selection acts on the phenotype (observable characteristics) not directly on the genotype.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is an example of commensalism?
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Barnacles on whales = commensalism (+/0). Mycorrhizae = mutualism. Plasmodium = parasitism.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 13 — Organisms and Populations, Section 13.3
questions.ncert_line: Barnacles on whales is commensalism — barnacles benefit (food, transport) while whales are unaffected.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The pyramid of energy is always upright because:

options.B: Energy is always lost at each trophic level

questions.explanation: Correct: B. Pyramid of energy is ALWAYS upright — energy lost at each level ensures producers have most.
questions.ncert_line: Energy is lost as heat at every trophic level (10% rule). So energy always decreases up the food chain.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The term 'biodiversity' was popularised by:
questions.explanation: Correct: A. E.O. Wilson popularised 'biodiversity'. Humboldt studied species-area relationships.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 15 — Biodiversity and Conservation, Section 15.1
questions.ncert_line: The term 'biological diversity' was popularised by sociobiologist E.O. Wilson in 1992.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is an example of IN SITU conservation?
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Biosphere reserve = in situ. Seed banks, zoos, botanical gardens = ex situ conservation.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 15 — Biodiversity and Conservation, Section 15.3
questions.ncert_line: In situ conservation = conservation in natural habitat. Biosphere reserves, national parks and sanctuaries are in situ methods.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The full form of ZIFT is:
questions.explanation: Correct: A. ZIFT = Zygote Intra Fallopian Transfer. GIFT = Gamete Intra Fallopian Transfer.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 4 — Reproductive Health, Section 4.3
questions.ncert_line: ZIFT = Zygote Intra Fallopian Transfer. The zygote is transferred into the fallopian tube.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The acrosome of a sperm is derived from:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Acrosome = modified Golgi body. Contains enzymes for fertilisation.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 3 — Human Reproduction, Section 3.2
questions.ncert_line: The acrosome is derived from the Golgi apparatus and contains hydrolytic enzymes for penetrating the egg.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The Graafian follicle ruptures and releases the ovum during:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Ovulation = rupture of Graafian follicle triggered by LH surge on day ~14.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 3 — Human Reproduction, Section 3.3
questions.ncert_line: Ovulation occurs around day 14 of a 28-day cycle. LH surge triggers rupture of Graafian follicle.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following hormones is NOT secreted by the placenta?
questions.explanation: Correct: D. FSH is NOT secreted by placenta. Placenta secretes hCG, estrogens, progestogens, hPL.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 3 — Human Reproduction, Section 3.4
questions.ncert_line: Placenta secretes hCG, estrogen, progesterone and hPL. FSH is secreted by anterior pituitary, not placenta.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: MTP stands for:
questions.explanation: Correct: A. MTP = Medical Termination of Pregnancy. Legal in India under MTP Act.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 4 — Reproductive Health, Section 4.2
questions.ncert_line: MTP = Medical Termination of Pregnancy. It is legal in India up to 20 weeks of pregnancy.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Double fertilisation is a characteristic feature of:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Double fertilisation is unique to angiosperms. Syngamy + triple fusion.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 2 — Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants, Section 2.7
questions.ncert_line: Double fertilisation — unique to angiosperms: one sperm + egg = zygote; other sperm + polar nuclei = endosperm.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The enzyme used to join two DNA fragments is:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. DNA ligase = molecular glue. Restriction enzymes cut; ligase joins.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 11 — Biotechnology Principles and Processes, Section 11.2
questions.ncert_line: DNA ligase seals the nicks and joins two DNA fragments by forming phosphodiester bonds.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Restriction endonucleases cut DNA at specific sequences called:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Restriction enzymes cut at palindromic sequences. Example: EcoRI cuts at GAATTC.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 11 — Biotechnology Principles and Processes, Section 11.2
questions.ncert_line: Restriction enzymes recognise specific palindromic sequences (read same in both directions 5'→3') and cut at or near them.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Golden Rice is a transgenic variety that is rich in:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Golden Rice has beta-carotene — a provitamin A. Developed to combat Vitamin A deficiency.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 12 — Biotechnology and its Applications, Section 12.2
questions.ncert_line: Golden Rice is engineered to produce beta-carotene (precursor of Vitamin A) in the endosperm.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The Bt toxin from Bacillus thuringiensis is coded by the gene:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. cry genes code for Bt toxin (crystal protein). nif = nitrogen fixation. bar = herbicide resistance.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 12 — Biotechnology and its Applications, Section 12.2
questions.ncert_line: The cry genes encode crystal proteins (Bt toxins). CryIAb controls corn borer; CryIAc controls cotton bollworm.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The first clinical gene therapy was given for the disease:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. ADA deficiency (SCID) — first gene therapy in 1990. ADA enzyme is critical for immune function.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 12 — Biotechnology and its Applications, Section 12.3
questions.ncert_line: The first gene therapy (1990) was for ADA deficiency (SCID). Functional ADA gene inserted into patient's lymphocytes.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The causative organism of malaria is:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Plasmodium causes malaria. Female Anopheles = vector. P. falciparum = most dangerous.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 8 — Human Health and Disease, Section 8.2
questions.ncert_line: Malaria is caused by Plasmodium (P. vivax, P. falciparum, P. malariae, P. ovale). Transmitted by female Anopheles mosquito.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The haemozoin released during malarial attack causes:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Haemozoin causes chill and fever. Released when RBCs rupture during merozoite release.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 8 — Human Health and Disease, Section 8.2
questions.ncert_line: When RBCs rupture, haemozoin (toxic pigment) is released causing chill and high fever characteristic of malaria.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Streptokinase produced by Streptoccus is used as:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Streptokinase = clot buster. Used in myocardial infarction treatment.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 10 — Microbes in Human Welfare, Section 10.4
questions.ncert_line: Streptokinase (clot buster / thrombolytic agent) produced by Streptoccus is used to dissolve blood clots in heart attack patients.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following microorganisms is used in the production of Swiss cheese?
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Propionibacterium shermanii makes Swiss cheese holes by producing CO₂.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 10 — Microbes in Human Welfare, Section 10.1
questions.ncert_line: Propionibacterium shermanii produces CO2 during Swiss cheese ripening, creating the characteristic holes.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is a secondary lymphoid organ?
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Spleen is a secondary lymphoid organ. Bone marrow and thymus are primary lymphoid organs.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 8 — Human Health and Disease, Section 8.1
questions.ncert_line: Secondary lymphoid organs: spleen, lymph nodes, tonsils, Peyer's patches, appendix. Primary: bone marrow, thymus.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The process by which pollen grains are transferred from anther to stigma is called:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Pollination = pollen transfer anther→stigma. Fertilisation = fusion of gametes.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 2 — Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants, Section 2.3
questions.ncert_line: Pollination is the transfer of pollen grains from the anther to the stigma of a flower.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a feature of cleistogamous flowers?
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Cleistogamous flowers are self-pollinated (not cross-pollinated). Example: Commelina, Viola.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 2 — Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants, Section 2.3
questions.ncert_line: Cleistogamous flowers never open and are therefore always self-pollinated, not cross-pollinated.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Double fertilisation in angiosperms involves fusion of:

options.A: Two sperms with two egg cells
options.B: One sperm with egg and one sperm with secondary nucleus
options.C: One sperm with two polar nuclei only
options.D: Two sperms with the central cell

questions.explanation: Correct: B. Syngamy: sperm + egg → zygote. Triple fusion: sperm + 2 polar nuclei → primary endosperm nucleus.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 2 — Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants, Section 2.5
questions.ncert_line: Double fertilisation: one sperm fuses with egg (syngamy) → zygote; second sperm fuses with secondary nucleus (triple fusion) → endosperm.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The process of release of ovum from the Graafian follicle is called:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ovulation = release of ovum from Graafian follicle. Occurs around day 14 of 28-day cycle.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 3 — Human Reproduction, Section 3.2
questions.ncert_line: Ovulation is the release of the secondary oocyte (ovum) from the Graafian follicle, triggered by LH surge.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The hormone responsible for maintaing the corpus luteum during early pregnancy is:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. hCG maintains corpus luteum → corpus luteum secretes progesterone → maintains uterine ling.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 3 — Human Reproduction, Section 3.3
questions.ncert_line: hCG (human chorionic gonadotropin) secreted by the trophoblast maintains corpus luteum during early pregnancy.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In gel electrophoresis, DNA fragments are separated based on:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Gel electrophoresis separates by size. Smaller = faster. Stained with ethidium bromide.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 11 — Biotechnology: Principles and Processes, Section 11.3
questions.ncert_line: DNA fragments migrate in agarose gel based on size — smaller fragments move faster toward positive electrode.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Bt toxin produced by Bacillus thuringiensis is a:

options.B: Protoxin (inactive form) activated in insect gut

questions.explanation: Correct: B. Bt toxin is a protoxin — inactive in bacteria, activated by alkaline insect midgut pH.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 12 — Biotechnology and its Applications, Section 12.1
questions.ncert_line: Bt toxin exists as inactive protoxin in bacteria. The alkaline pH of insect gut activates it by solubilising.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The first transgenic cow produced to give human protein in its milk was:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Rosie — first transgenic cow with human alpha-lactalbumin in milk. Dolly was cloned sheep.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 12 — Biotechnology and its Applications, Section 12.2
questions.ncert_line: Rosie was the first transgenic cow that produced human protein-enriched milk (human alpha-lactalbumin).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Gene therapy was first used to treat:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. First gene therapy — ADA deficiency (1990). ADA enzyme essential for immune function.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 12 — Biotechnology and its Applications, Section 12.3
questions.ncert_line: The first clinical gene therapy was used in 1990 for ADA deficiency (SCID) — lymphocytes were given functional ADA gene.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The drug colchicine inhibits cell division by:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Colchicine inhibits spindle formation → arrests cell division at metaphase. Used to make polyploids.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 8 — Human Health and Disease, Section 8.4
questions.ncert_line: Colchicine binds to tubulin and prevents polymerisation of spindle fibres — arrests mitosis at metaphase.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Streptokinase used as a clot buster is produced by:
questions.explanation: Correct: A. Streptokinase from Streptoccus. Used to treat myocardial infarction (heart attack).
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 10 — Microbes in Human Welfare, Section 10.2
questions.ncert_line: Streptokinase is produced by Streptoccus bacteria. It is used as a thrombolytic drug to dissolve blood clots.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Cyclosporin A, an immunosuppressant used in organ transplants, is produced by:
questions.explanation: Correct: A. Cyclosporin A — Trichoderma polysporum. Statins (cholesterol-lowering) — Monascus pureus.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 10 — Microbes in Human Welfare, Section 10.2
questions.ncert_line: Cyclosporin A is produced by the fungus Trichoderma polysporum. Used to prevent organ rejection.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In a monohybrid cross between Tt × Tt, the phenotypic ratio is:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Phenotypic ratio in Tt × Tt = 3:1 (dominant:recessive).
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 5 — Principles of Inheritance and Variation, Section 5.1
questions.ncert_line: Tt × Tt gives genotypes TT:Tt:tt = 1:2:1. Since T is dominant, TT and Tt show same phenotype. Phenotypic ratio = 3:1.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is an example of co-dominance?
questions.explanation: Correct: A. ABO blood group = co-dominance. IA and IB are co-dominant (both expressed in IAIB individual).
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 5 — Principles of Inheritance and Variation, Section 5.2
questions.ncert_line: ABO blood groups show co-dominance — IA and IB alles are both expressed in AB blood group.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The enzyme that unwinds the DNA double helix during replication is:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Helicase unwinds DNA. DNA polymerase adds nucleotides. Ligase joins fragments. Primase makes RNA primer.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 6 — Molecular Basis of Inheritance, Section 6.3
questions.ncert_line: Helicase breaks hydrogen bonds between complementary bases to unwind and separate the two strands.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In E. coli, the lac operon is induced by:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Allolactose (derived from lactose) is the inducer — inactivates repressor → lac operon transcribed.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 6 — Molecular Basis of Inheritance, Section 6.6
questions.ncert_line: Allolactose (a metabolite of lactose) binds the lac repressor and inactivates it, allowing transcription of lac genes.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Industrial melanism in Biston betularia moth is an example of:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Industrial melanism = natural selection. Melanic moths had survival advantage on dark tree bark.
questions.ncert_line: Dark moths survived better on soot-covered trees (camouflage) → natural selection favoured melanic forms.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The concept of 'Punctuated Equilibrium' was proposed by:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Gould and Eldredge proposed punctuated equilibrium (1972).
questions.ncert_line: Punctuated equilibrium (rapid evolution interspersed with long stable periods) was proposed by Gould and Eldredge.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The vaccine for smallpox was developed by:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Edward Jenner — smallpox vaccine (1796). First vaccine ever developed.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 8 — Human Health and Disease, Section 8.1
questions.ncert_line: Edward Jenner developed the smallpox vaccine in 1796 using cowpox virus (vaccinia).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Heterospory (production of two kinds of spores) is first seen in:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Heterospory first appears in pteridophytes (Selaginella). Leads to evolution of seed habit.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 3 — Plant Kingdom, Section 3.3
questions.ncert_line: Heterospory first evolved in pteridophytes (e.g. Selaginella, Salvinia). This is considered a precursor to seed habit.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which gymnosperm is called a 'living fossil'?
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ginkgo biloba = living fossil. Cycas = common gymnosperm. Pinus = pine tree.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 3 — Plant Kingdom, Section 3.4
questions.ncert_line: Ginkgo biloba is called a living fossil — it is the only surving species of the order Ginkgoales.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Venation in monocot leaves is:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Monocots = parallel venation. Dicots = reticulate venation.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 5 — Morphology of Flowering Plants, Section 5.3
questions.ncert_line: Monocot leaves have parallel venation. Dicot leaves have reticulate (network) venation.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a characteristic of a tap root system?
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Fibrous and adventious roots = monocot feature. Tap root = dicot, from radicle.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 5 — Morphology of Flowering Plants, Section 5.1
questions.ncert_line: Fibrous and adventious roots are features of monocots, not tap root systems.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The tissue responsible for lateral growth (secondary growth) in dicot stems is:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Vascular cambium = secondary growth. Produces wood (secondary xylem) and bast (secondary phloem).
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 6 — Anatomy of Flowering Plants, Section 6.4
questions.ncert_line: Vascular cambium (lateral meristem) produces secondary xylem inward and secondary phloem outward.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The relationship between clownfish and sea anemone is an example of:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Clownfish + sea anemone = mutualism (+/+). Both benefit from the relationship.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 13 — Organisms and Populations, Section 13.3
questions.ncert_line: Clownfish gets shelter and protection from sea anemone; anemone benefits from waste nutrients and cleaning by clownfish — mutual benefit.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following correctly represents a sigmoid (logistic) growth curve?

options.B: J-shaped curve with no carrying capacity
options.C: S-shaped curve with upper asymptote at K
options.D: Linear growth followed by sudden decline

questions.explanation: Correct: C. Logistic growth = S-shaped curve. Upper asymptote = K (carrying capacity).
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 13 — Organisms and Populations, Section 13.4
questions.ncert_line: Logistic growth is S-shaped (sigmoid). Population grows slowly, then rapidly, then levels off at carrying capacity K.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a characteristic of a pioneer species in primary succession?

options.B: They modify the environment for future species
options.C: They have high competive ability in stable environments

questions.explanation: Correct: C. Pioneers have LOW competive ability. They are r-strategists — good at colonising, poor at competing.
questions.ncert_line: Pioneer species are stress-tolerant but NOT highly competive — they are replaced by more competive species as succession proceeds.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: BOD (Biochemical Oxygen Demand) is a measure of:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. High BOD = high organic pollution. Clean water has BOD < 5 ppm.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 16 — Environmental Issues, Section 16.2
questions.ncert_line: BOD measures the amount of dissolved oxygen need by microorganisms to decompose organic matter in water.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The functional unit of the kidney is:
questions.explanation: Correct: A. Nephron = functional unit. Each kidney has ~1 million nephrons.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 19 — Excretory Products and their Elimination, Section 19.1
questions.ncert_line: The nephron is the structural and functional unit of the kidney. Each kidney has about 1 million nephrons.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The process of releasing urine from the urinary bladder is called:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Micturition = urination. Controlled by both voluntary and involuntary mechanisms.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 19 — Excretory Products and their Elimination, Section 19.3
questions.ncert_line: Micturition (urination) is the process of releasing stored urine from the urinary bladder through the urethra.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The protein that forms the thin filament in muscle is:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Actin = thin filament. Myosin = thick filament. Ca²⁺ binds troponin to expose actin binding sites.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 20 — Locomotion and Movement, Section 20.2
questions.ncert_line: Thin filaments are composed of actin (F-actin), troponin and tropomyosin. Myosin forms thick filaments.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which hormone is called the 'emergency hormone' or 'hormone of fight or flight'?
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Adrenaline = fight or flight hormone. Secreted by adrenal medulla.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 22 — Chemical Coordination and Integration, Section 22.3
questions.ncert_line: Adrenaline (epinephrine) secreted by adrenal medulla prepares body for fight or flight — increases heart rate, dilates pupils.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Diabetes insipidus is caused by deficiency of:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. ADH deficiency → diabetes insipidus (not mellitus). Diabetes mellitus = insulin deficiency.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 22 — Chemical Coordination and Integration, Section 22.2
questions.ncert_line: ADH (antidiuretic hormone) promotes water reabsorption in kidneys. Deficiency → excess dilute urine = diabetes insipidus.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The powerhouse of the cell is:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Mitochondria = powerhouse. Site of Krebs cycle and oxidative phosphorylation.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 8 — Cell: The Unit of Life, Section 8.4
questions.ncert_line: Mitochondria produce ATP via oxidative phosphorylation — hence called the powerhouse of the cell.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The 9+2 arrangement of microtubules is found in:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Cilia/flagella = 9+2 axoneme. Centrioles = 9+0 (triplets, no central tubules).
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 8 — Cell: The Unit of Life, Section 8.5
questions.ncert_line: Cilia and flagella have 9+2 arrangement: 9 peripheral doublet microtubules + 2 central singlet microtubules (axoneme).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which organelle is responsible for modifying, sorting and packaging proteins for secretion?
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Golgi apparatus = modification + packaging + secretion. ER = synthesis of proteins and lipids.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 8 — Cell: The Unit of Life, Section 8.6
questions.ncert_line: Golgi apparatus (Golgi complex) is the cell's post office — modifies proteins from ER and packages them for secretion.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Synapsis (pairing of homologous chromosomes) occurs during which sub-stage of Prophase I?
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Zygotene = synapsis. Leptotene = chromosomes first visible. Pachytene = crossing over.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 10 — Cell Cycle and Cell Division, Section 10.3
questions.ncert_line: Synapsis (formation of synaptonemal complex between homologous chromosomes) occurs during zygotene.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The DNA content per cell in G2 phase compared to G1 phase is:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. G2 DNA = 2 × G1 DNA. S phase replication doubles DNA content.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 10 — Cell Cycle and Cell Division, Section 10.1
questions.ncert_line: S phase (between G1 and G2) replicates DNA. So G2 has double the DNA content of G1.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following growth models shows a J-shaped curve?
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Exponential growth = J-shaped curve. Logistic growth = S-shaped (sigmoidal) curve with K as upper limit.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 13 — Organisms and Populations, Section 13.4
questions.ncert_line: Exponential (geometric) growth: dN/dt = rN gives J-shaped curve with no upper limit.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The interaction where both species are harmed is called:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Competion = both harmed (−/−). Gause's competive exclusion principle applies here.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 13 — Organisms and Populations, Section 13.3
questions.ncert_line: Competion (−/−): both species are harmed. Mutualism (+/+), Predation (+/−), Commensalism (+/0).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The 10% energy transfer law in a food chain was given by:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Lindemann's 10% law. Only 10% energy passes to next trophic level; 90% lost as heat.
questions.ncert_line: Lindemann (1942) proposed the 10% law — only 10% of energy at one trophic level transfers to the next.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a greenhouse gas?
questions.explanation: Correct: D. N₂ is not a greenhouse gas. It makes up 78% of atmosphere but doesn't trap heat.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 16 — Environmental Issues, Section 16.4
questions.ncert_line: N2 is NOT a greenhouse gas. CO2, CH4, N2O, H2O vapour and CFCs are greenhouse gases.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The IUCN Red List categorises a species as 'Extinct in the Wild' when:

options.A: It survives only in captivity or cultivation
options.C: It cannot be located in its habitat
options.D: It has fewer than 50 mature individuals

questions.explanation: Correct: A. Extinct in Wild = survives only in captivity/cultivation. Critically Endangered = <50 mature individuals.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 15 — Biodiversity and Conservation, Section 15.3
questions.ncert_line: IUCN category 'Extinct in the Wild' = species survives only in captivity or as naturalised population outside its range.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Absorption of glucose and amino acids in the small intestine occurs by:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Active transport with Na+ gradient. Fatty acids absorbed differently — as micelles into lymph.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 16 — Digestion and Absorption, Section 16.4
questions.ncert_line: Glucose and amino acids are absorbed by active transport using Na+ co-transport across intestinal epithelium.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The tidal volume of a normal adult at rest is approximately:
questions.explanation: Correct: A. Tidal volume ≈ 500 mL. IRV ≈ 2500 mL. ERV ≈ 1000 mL. RV ≈ 1100 mL.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 17 — Breathing and Exchange of Gases, Section 17.2
questions.ncert_line: Tidal volume (TV) = volume of air inspired/expired in one normal breath ≈ 500 mL.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The blood group that is called 'universal donor' is:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. O− = universal donor. AB+ = universal recipient.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 18 — Body Fluids and Circulation, Section 18.2
questions.ncert_line: O− (O negative) is the universal donor — no A, B antigens and no Rh factor, so can donate to any blood group.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The protein absent in smooth muscle compared to striated muscle is:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Smooth muscle lacks troponin. Ca²⁺ regulation differs — uses calmodulin instead.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 20 — Locomotion and Movement, Section 20.2
questions.ncert_line: Smooth muscle lacks troponin. Striated (skeletal/cardiac) muscle has actin, myosin AND troponin-tropomyosin complex.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The hormone that regulates blood calcium levels is:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. PTH raises blood Ca²⁺. Calcitonin (thyroid) lowers blood Ca²⁺. They are antagonistic.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 22 — Chemical Coordination and Integration, Section 22.4
questions.ncert_line: PTH (parathormone) from parathyroid glands increases blood Ca²⁺ by stimulating bone resorption and kidney reabsorption.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which type of epithelium lines the inner surface of blood vessels?
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Endothelium (simple squamous) lines blood vessels. Allows smooth blood flow.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 7 — Structural Organisation in Animals, Section 7.2
questions.ncert_line: Blood vessels are lined by simple squamous epithelium called endothelium — allows easy diffusion.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Cartilage is an example of which type of tissue?
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Cartilage = connective tissue. Chondrocytes in lacunae within matrix.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 7 — Structural Organisation in Animals, Section 7.3
questions.ncert_line: Cartilage is a specialised connective tissue with chondrocytes embedded in a matrix of chondroitin sulphate.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The respiratory organs of cockroach are:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Cockroach breathes through tracheae (not lungs). Book lungs = spiders. Gills = aquatic organisms.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 7 — Structural Organisation in Animals, Section 7.4
questions.ncert_line: Cockroaches breathe through tracheae (network of tubes) that open via spiracles on the body surface.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Assertion (A): DNA replication is semi-conservative. Reason (R): Each new DNA molecule contains one old and one new strand.

options.A: A is true, R is true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: A is true, R is true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true, R is false
options.D: A is false, R is true

questions.explanation: Correct: A. Both true and R explains A. Meselson-Stahl experiment (1958) confirmed semi-conservative replication.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 6 — Molecular Basis of Inheritance, Section 6.3
questions.ncert_line: Semi-conservative replication (proved by Meselson-Stahl): each daughter DNA has one parental + one new strand. R correctly explains A.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Assertion (A): C4 plants are more efficient in hot, sunny climates. Reason (R): C4 plants avoid photorespiration by concentrating CO₂ in bundle sheath cells.

options.A: A is true, R is true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: A is true, R is true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true, R is false
options.D: A is false, R is true

questions.explanation: Correct: A. Both true and R explains A. C4 plants have CO₂-concentrating mechanism eliminating photorespiration.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 13 — Photosynthesis in Higher Plants, Section 13.4
questions.ncert_line: C4 plants (maize, sugarcane) use PEP carboxylase in mesophyll to fix CO2 into C4 acids, then release CO2 in bundle sheath — bypassing photorespiration.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Assertion (A): Myelinated nerve fibres conduct impulses faster than unmyelinated fibres. Reason (R): In myelinated fibres, impulse jumps from one node of Ranvier to the next (saltatory conduction).

options.A: A is true, R is true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: A is true, R is true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true, R is false
options.D: A is false, R is true

questions.explanation: Correct: A. Both true, R correctly explains A. Saltatory conduction = faster impulse in myelinated fibres.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 21 — Neural Control and Coordination, Section 21.2
questions.ncert_line: Saltatory conduction in myelinated fibres: impulse jumps node to node (skipping myelin sheath) — much faster than continuous conduction.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Assertion (A): Wind-pollinated flowers are generally small and inconspicuous. Reason (R): Wind-pollinated flowers do not need to attract pollinators.

options.A: A is true, R is true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: A is true, R is true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true, R is false
options.D: A is false, R is true

questions.explanation: Correct: A. Both true and R explains A. Wind pollination: light pollen, feathery stigma, no colourful petals.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 2 — Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants, Section 2.3
questions.ncert_line: Anemophilous (wind-pollinated) flowers: small, light pollen, no nectar/fragrance — no need to attract pollinators.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Assertion (A): Decomposers are essential for nutrient cycling in an ecosystem. Reason (R): Decomposers convert complex organic matter into inorganic nutrients available to producers.

options.A: A is true, R is true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: A is true, R is true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true, R is false
options.D: A is false, R is true

questions.explanation: Correct: A. Both true, R explains A. Without decomposers, nutrients would remain locked in dead organic matter.
questions.ncert_line: Decomposers (bacteria, fungi) mineralise organic matter → release inorganic nutrients → used by producers. Essential for nutrient cycling.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Viruses are NOT included in any kingdom of the five-kingdom classification because:

options.B: They are acellular and can replicate only inside a host cell
options.D: They have no metabolism of their own when outside a host

questions.explanation: Correct: B. Viruses = acellular, obligate intracellular. Neither living nor non-living — they are placed outside five kingdoms.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 2 — Biological Classification, Section 2.5
questions.ncert_line: Viruses are acellular (no cell structure) and are obligate intracellular parasites — they don't fit any kingdom.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is a macronutrient required by plants?
questions.explanation: Correct: D. Magnesium is a macronutrient (part of chlorophyll). Fe, Mn, Zn are micronutrients. Common exam trap.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 12 — Mineral Nutrition, Section 12.2
questions.ncert_line: Macronutrients: C, H, O, N, P, K, Ca, Mg, S. Iron, Mn, Zn are micronutrients. Mg is a macronutrient (in chlorophyll).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The enzyme that is called a 'biological catalyst' works by:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Enzymes LOWER activation energy. They do NOT change equilibrium or provide energy.
questions.ncert_line: Enzymes lower the activation energy barrier — more substrate molecules can overcome it, speeding up the reaction.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Insulin is produced by which cells of the pancreas?
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Beta cells → insulin. Alpha cells → glucagon. Common confusion in exams.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 22 — Chemical Coordination and Integration, Section 22.5
questions.ncert_line: Beta cells of islets of Langerhans produce insulin. Alpha cells produce glucagon. Delta cells produce somatostatin.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The plant hormone that promotes cell elongation and is responsible for photropism is:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Auxin causes photropism. Unequal distribution → shaded side elongates → bending toward light.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 15 — Plant Growth and Development, Section 15.3
questions.ncert_line: Auxin (IAA) promotes cell elongation. In photropism, auxin migrates to the shaded side causing unequal growth.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Bolting (elongation of internodes before flowering) is promoted by:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Gibberellin → bolting. Also promotes seed germination and fruit development.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 15 — Plant Growth and Development, Section 15.4
questions.ncert_line: Gibberellins promote bolting (rapid stem elongation) in rosette plants like cabbage before flowering.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which plant hormone is called the 'stress hormone' because it promotes seed dormancy and stomatal closure?
questions.explanation: Correct: D. ABA = stress hormone. Closes stomata, promotes dormancy, inhibits seed germination.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 15 — Plant Growth and Development, Section 15.5
questions.ncert_line: ABA = abscisic acid is the stress hormone. Promotes stomatal closure during water stress, inhibits growth.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The mechanism of opening of stomata is due to:

options.A: Decrease in osmotic pressure of guard cells
options.B: Increase in turgor pressure of guard cells
options.C: Decrease in turgor pressure of guard cells
options.D: Loss of water from guard cells

questions.explanation: Correct: B. Turgor pressure increases in guard cells → stomata open. K+ uptake drives water in.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 11 — Transport in Plants, Section 11.4
questions.ncert_line: Stomata open when guard cells accumulate K+ ions → water enters by osmosis → turgor increases → stomata open.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Nitrogen fixation by Rhizobium occurs in root nodules in the presence of enzyme:
questions.explanation: Correct: A. Nitrogenase fixes N₂ → NH₃ in root nodules. Leghaemoglobin maintains low O₂ to protect nitrogenase.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 12 — Mineral Nutrition, Section 12.4
questions.ncert_line: Nitrogenase enzyme complex (in Rhizobium) catalyses: N2 + 8H+ + 8e- → 2NH3 + H2. Requires anaerobic conditions.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The number of ATP molecules produced by complete oxidation of one glucose molecule (via aerobic respiration) is approximately:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. ~38 ATP from aerobic respiration. 2 from glycolysis, 2 from Krebs cycle, 34 from electron transport chain.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 14 — Respiration in Plants, Section 14.3
questions.ncert_line: Aerobic respiration: ~38 ATP (glycolysis: 2, Krebs: 2, oxidative phosphorylation: 34) from one glucose molecule.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The Calvin cycle (dark reactions) occurs in which part of the chloroplast?
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Calvin cycle = stroma. Light reactions = thylakoid membrane. ATP+NADPH from light reactions used in stroma.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 13 — Photosynthesis in Higher Plants, Section 13.3
questions.ncert_line: Calvin cycle (dark reactions/C3 pathway) occurs in the stroma of chloroplast. Light reactions occur in thylakoids.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Nematocysts (sting cells) are found in which phylum?
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Nematocysts in Cnidaria (Hydra, jellyfish, sea anemone). Sponges have spicules/spongin.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 4 — Animal Kingdom, Section 4.1
questions.ncert_line: Cnidarians (coelenterates) have cnidoblasts/cnidocytes containg nematocysts used for prey capture and defence.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which is the correct sequence of larval stages in frog development?
questions.explanation: Correct: A. Standard sequence: zygote → morula → blastula → gastrula → tadpole.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 4 — Animal Kingdom, Section 4.2
questions.ncert_line: Frog development: zygote → morula (cleavage) → blastula → gastrula → neurula → tadpole → metamorphosis → adult.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Flame cells are the excretory organs in:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Flame cells = excretory organs of Platyhelminthes. Nephridia = annelids. Malpighian tubules = insects.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 4 — Animal Kingdom, Section 4.1
questions.ncert_line: Platyhelminthes (flatworms) excrete using flame cells (protonephridia). Annelids use nephridia. Insects use Malpighian tubules.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The template strand of DNA is also called:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Template strand = antisense = non-coding strand. mRNA is complementary to it.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 6 — Molecular Basis of Inheritance, Section 6.4
questions.ncert_line: Template strand = antisense strand = non-coding strand. mRNA sequence matches coding (sense) strand.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The edible part of conut that we drink (conut water) represents:
questions.explanation: Correct: A. Coconut water = liquid endosperm. Coconut flesh = solid endosperm (both are endosperm).
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 2 — Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants, Section 2.6
questions.ncert_line: Coconut water = liquid nuclear endosperm. Coconut flesh = solid cellular endosperm.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Spermatogenesis occurs in:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Seminiferous tubules = site of spermatogenesis. Epidymis = maturation and storage.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 3 — Human Reproduction, Section 3.1
questions.ncert_line: Spermatogenesis (formation of sperms) occurs in seminiferous tubules of the tes.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which contraceptive method has the HIGHEST failure rate?
questions.explanation: Correct: D. Rhythm/calendar method = least reliable. OCP and IUD are most reliable among common methods.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 4 — Reproductive Health, Section 4.2
questions.ncert_line: Rhythm method (avoiding sex during fertile period) has highest failure rate ~20-25% due to irregular cycles.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which plant hormone is responsible for apical dominance?
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Auxin causes apical dominance. Removing apex (decapitation) allows lateral bud growth.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 15 — Plant Growth and Development, Section 15.3
questions.ncert_line: Auxin (IAA) produced at apical bud suppresses growth of lateral buds = apical dominance.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Bolting (premature elongation of stem before flowering) in rosette plants is caused by:
questions.explanation: Correct: D. Gibberellins cause bolting. Also involved in breaking seed dormancy and fruit development.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 15 — Plant Growth and Development, Section 15.3
questions.ncert_line: Gibberellins cause bolting — rapid internode elongation in rosette plants like cabbage under long days.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The deficiency of which element causes 'little leaf disease' in plants?
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Zinc deficiency → little leaf disease. Iron deficiency → interveinal chlorosis.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 12 — Mineral Nutrition, Section 12.3
questions.ncert_line: Zinc deficiency causes little leaf disease (small, distorted leaves) and khaira disease in rice.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Mycorrhiza is a symbiotic association between fungi and:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Mycorrhiza = fungi + plant roots. Lichen = fungi + algae (or cyanobacteria).
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 2 — Biological Classification, Section 2.2
questions.ncert_line: Mycorrhiza = mutualistic association between fungi and roots of higher plants. Fungi help in mineral absorption.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: TMV (Tobacco Mosaic Virus) was first crystallised by:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. W.M. Stanley crystallised TMV (1935). Ivanowsky showed infectious agent passes through filter (1892).
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 2 — Biological Classification, Section 2.6
questions.ncert_line: W.M. Stanley crystallised TMV and showed viruses are nucleoproteins. Ivanowsky first discovered virus filterability.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a characteristic of viruses?
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Viruses have no metabolism outside host. They are essentially nucleoprotein packages.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 2 — Biological Classification, Section 2.6
questions.ncert_line: Viruses have NO independent metabolism — they are inert outside a host cell and cannot synthesise ATP.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Flame cells are the excretory structures in:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Flame cells = Platyhelminthes. Nephridia = Annelids. Malpighian tubules = Insects.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 4 — Animal Kingdom, Section 4.1
questions.ncert_line: Flame cells (protonephridia) are excretory structures in flatworms (Platyhelminthes) like Planaria.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The larva of sea squirt (Urochordata) has a notochord but the adult does not. This is called:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Retrogressive metamorphosis in Urochordata — larva has notochord, adult loses it.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 4 — Animal Kingdom, Section 4.2
questions.ncert_line: Retrogressive metamorphosis: larva is more complex (has notochord) than the degenerate sessile adult.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The main driving force for water movement up the xylem is:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Cohesion-tension theory — transpiration pull is the main force for ascent of sap.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 11 — Transport in Plants, Section 11.4
questions.ncert_line: Transpiration pull: evaporation from leaves creates tension that pulls water column up through xylem (cohesion-tension theory).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which mineral element is need for nitrogen fixation in Rhizobium?
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Molybdenum is part of nitrogenase. Also need: Fe (in Fe-protein component of nitrogenase).
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 12 — Mineral Nutrition, Section 12.4
questions.ncert_line: Molybdenum is a component of nitrogenase enzyme (Mo-Fe protein) essential for biological nitrogen fixation.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The Z-scheme of photosynthesis involves:

options.C: Both PS I and PS II in sequence

questions.explanation: Correct: C. Z-scheme involves PS II → electron transport chain → PS I. Generates ATP and NADPH.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 13 — Photosynthesis in Higher Plants, Section 13.3
questions.ncert_line: Z-scheme: light energy absorbed by PS II → electrons flow to PS I → NADPH produced. Both systems work in series.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Abscisic acid (ABA) is called the 'stress hormone' because it:

options.B: Causes stomatal closure and seed dormancy during stress

questions.explanation: Correct: B. ABA = stress hormone. Closes stomata in drought, promotes dormancy. Antagonises GA.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 15 — Plant Growth and Development, Section 15.5
questions.ncert_line: ABA causes stomatal closure (conserves water) and promotes seed dormancy. It counteracts gibberellins.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Vernalisation refers to:

options.B: Requirement of low temperature for flowering

questions.explanation: Correct: B. Vernalisation = cold treatment need for flowering. Example: winter wheat needs cold period.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 15 — Plant Growth and Development, Section 15.6
questions.ncert_line: Vernalisation: low temperature treatment required by certain plants (e.g. wheat) before they can flower.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In a dihybrid cross between AaBb × AaBb, the phenotypic ratio is:
questions.explanation: Correct: A. Dihybrid ratio = 9:3:1 when genes are on different chromosomes (independent assortment).
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 5 — Principles of Inheritance and Variation, Section 5.1
questions.ncert_line: Standard dihybrid cross AaBb × AaBb gives phenotypic ratio 9:3:1 (9 A_B_ : 3 A_bb : 3 aaB_ : 1 aabb).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: A man with blood group A (IAi) marries a woman with blood group B (IBi). What fraction of their children can have blood group O?
questions.explanation: Correct: B. 1/4 children will be ii (blood group O). ABO = multiple alle inheritance.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 5 — Principles of Inheritance and Variation, Section 5.2
questions.ncert_line: IAi × IBi: offspring genotypes IAIB (AB), IAi (A), IBi (B), ii (O) in ratio 1:1. So 1/4 chance of O.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Chargaff's rule states that in DNA:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. A = T and G = C (complementary base pairing). This confirmed Watson-Crick double helix model.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 6 — Molecular Basis of Inheritance, Section 6.2
questions.ncert_line: Chargaff's rule: A pairs with T (A = T) and G pairs with C (G = C). Also A+G = T+C (purines = pyrimidines).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The process of synthesis of RNA from DNA template is called:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Transcription = DNA → RNA. Translation = RNA → Protein. Both together = gene expression.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 6 — Molecular Basis of Inheritance, Section 6.4
questions.ncert_line: Transcription: DNA → RNA (using RNA polymerase). Translation: RNA → Protein. Replication: DNA → DNA.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which enzyme adds a poly-A tail to eukaryotic mRNA during post-transcriptional modification?
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Poly-A polymerase adds poly-A tail at 3' end. 5' cap (7-methylguanosine) is added at 5' end.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 6 — Molecular Basis of Inheritance, Section 6.5
questions.ncert_line: Poly-A polymerase adds ~200 adenine residues to the 3' end of pre-mRNA — this poly-A tail increases mRNA stability.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following provides the strongest evidence for evolution?
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Homologous organs = strongest evidence (divergent evolution from common ancestor). Analogous = convergent evolution.
questions.ncert_line: Homologous organs (same fundamental structure, different functions) provide strongest evidence for common ancestry and divergent evolution.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The acrosome reaction in sperm involves:

options.B: Release of hydrolytic enzymes to penetrate zona pellucida

questions.explanation: Correct: B. Acrosome reaction = enzyme release to penetrate zona pellucida. Occurs before fertilisation.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 3 — Human Reproduction, Section 3.4
questions.ncert_line: Acrosome reaction: acrosome (lysome-like cap on sperm) releases hydrolytic enzymes to digest zona pellucida.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Cu-T (copper-T) IUD acts as a contraceptive primarily because copper ions:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Cu-T: copper ions are spermicidal + prevent implantation. Makes uterus hostile.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 4 — Reproductive Health, Section 4.2
questions.ncert_line: Copper ions are spermicidal and IUD creates hostile environment preventing implantation of fertilised egg.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The bond that links two amino acids in a protein is called:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Peptide bond = -CO-NH- between amino acids. Formed by condensation (dehydration).
questions.ncert_line: Peptide bond (-CO-NH-) forms between carboxyl group of one amino acid and amino group of next, with loss of water.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is a competive inhibitor of an enzyme?

options.A: It permanently destroys the active site
options.B: It binds to allosteric site and changes active site shape
options.C: It resembles the substrate and binds reversibly to active site

questions.explanation: Correct: C. Competive inhibitor competes with substrate for active site. Overcome by excess substrate.
questions.ncert_line: Competive inhibitor resembles substrate, binds reversibly to active site. Effect reversed by increasing substrate concentration.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a function of lipids?
questions.explanation: Correct: D. Genetic information = nucleic acids. Lipids: energy, membrane structure, steroid hormones, insulation.
questions.ncert_line: Genetic information is stored in nucleic acids (DNA/RNA), not lipids. Lipids function in energy storage, membranes and hormones.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The Km value of an enzyme represents:

options.B: Substrate concentration at half maximum velocity
options.D: pH at which enzyme is most active

questions.explanation: Correct: B. Km = [S] giving half-maximal velocity. Low Km → high enzyme-substrate affinity.
questions.ncert_line: Km (Michaelis constant) = [S] at which reaction rate = Vmax/2. Low Km = high affinity for substrate.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Insulin is a protein hormone. Its structure is maintained by:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Insulin chains held by disulphide (-S-) bonds between cysteine residues.
questions.ncert_line: Insulin has two chains (A and B) connected by disulphide bonds between cysteine residues. These bonds maintain tertiary structure.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The nitrogen cycle involves which of the following processes in correct order for conversion of N₂ to NO₃−?
questions.explanation: Correct: B. N₂ → NH₃ (fixation) → NO₂− → NO₃− (nitrification). Reverse = denitrification.
questions.ncert_line: N2 → NH3/NH4+ (nitrogen fixation by Rhizobium) → NO2− (nitrification by Nitrosomonas) → NO3− (by Nitrobacter).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Ozone depletion in the stratosphere is mainly caused by:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. CFCs → Cl• radicals → catalytic O₃ destruction. One Cl• can destroy 1 lakh O₃ molecules.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 16 — Environmental Issues, Section 16.4
questions.ncert_line: CFCs release Cl• radicals in stratosphere. Cl• catalytically destroys ozone: Cl• + O3 → ClO• + O2.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Eutrophication is caused by excess nutrients in water bodies. The immediate result is:

options.B: Algal bloom reducing oxygen for other organisms

questions.explanation: Correct: B. Eutrophication: nutrient excess → algal bloom → O₂ depletion → aquatic death (hypoxia).
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 16 — Environmental Issues, Section 16.2
questions.ncert_line: Excess nutrients (N, P) → algal bloom → decomposition consumes O2 → hypoxia → death of aquatic organisms.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is a viral disease?
questions.explanation: Correct: C. AIDS = HIV (retrovirus). HIV attacks CD4+ T-helper cells, collapsing immune system.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 8 — Human Health and Disease, Section 8.2
questions.ncert_line: AIDS is caused by HIV (Human Immunodeficiency Virus). Typhoid = Salmonella (bacteria). Malaria = Plasmodium (protozoa).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Biogas is produced by anaerobic digestion of organic matter. The main component of biogas is:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Biogas = mainly CH₄ (methane). Produced by methanogenic bacteria in anaerobic conditions.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 10 — Microbes in Human Welfare, Section 10.4
questions.ncert_line: Biogas is primarily methane (CH4, ~50-70%), CO2 (~30%), traces of H2S. Produced by methanogenic archaea.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Primary productivity is highest in which of the following ecosystems?
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Tropical rainforest = highest NPP (~2000 g/m²/yr). Desert = lowest (<200 g/m²/yr).
questions.ncert_line: Tropical rainforests have the highest net primary productivity due to year-round sunlight, high temperatures, and abundant rainfall.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Decomposers in an ecosystem primarily break down:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Decomposers = break down detritus. Also called saprotrophs or saprobes.
questions.ncert_line: Decomposers (bacteria and fungi) break down dead organic matter (detritus) into inorganic nutrients, completing nutrient cycles.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The full form of BOD is:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. BOD = Biochemical Oxygen Demand. High BOD = heavily polluted water (more microbes breaking down organic matter).
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 16 — Environmental Issues, Section 16.2
questions.ncert_line: BOD = Biochemical Oxygen Demand. It measures the amount of dissolved oxygen consumed by biological organisms in water.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Eutrophication of water bodies is caused by excessive:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Eutrophication caused by N and P (fertiliser runoff). Leads to algal bloom and oxygen depletion.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 16 — Environmental Issues, Section 16.2
questions.ncert_line: Eutrophication: excess N and P from agricultural runoff causes algal blooms → algae die → decomposition depletes O2 → aquatic life dies.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Carrying capacity (K) in logistic growth is defined as:

options.B: Maximum population size that an environment can support

questions.explanation: Correct: B. K = carrying capacity. At K, dN/dt = 0 (no net growth). Logistic equation: dN/dt = rN(K-N)/K.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 13 — Organisms and Populations, Section 13.4
questions.ncert_line: Carrying capacity K = maximum population size the environment can sustain with available resources.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The bacterium used in the production of biogas (methane) is:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Methanobacterium produces methane (CH₄) in anaerobic conditions. These are archaebacteria.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 10 — Microbes in Human Welfare, Section 10.4
questions.ncert_line: Methanobacterium (methanogens) are anaerobic bacteria that produce methane (biogas) from organic matter.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Primary and secondary sewage treatment differs in that secondary treatment:

options.B: Uses biological treatment with microorganisms to reduce BOD

questions.explanation: Correct: B. Secondary (biological) treatment reduces BOD significantly. Primary = physical settling only.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 10 — Microbes in Human Welfare, Section 10.5
questions.ncert_line: Secondary treatment uses activated sludge (aerobic microorganisms) to break down dissolved organic matter, greatly reducing BOD.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Prokaryotic cells are generally smaller than eukaryotic cells. Which of the following is a correct reason?

options.B: Prokaryotes lack membrane-bound organelles, allowing simpler smaller organisation
options.C: Prokaryotes divide faster so stay small

questions.explanation: Correct: B. No membrane-bound organelles = simpler organisation = smaller size. Eukaryotes need organelles for compartmentalisation.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 8 — Cell: The Unit of Life, Section 8.2
questions.ncert_line: Prokaryotes lack membrane-bound organelles and nucleus. This simpler organisation permits a smaller cell size.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The middle lamella between adjacent plant cells is mainly composed of:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Middle lamella = calcium pectate. Primary wall = cellulose. Secondary wall = cellulose + lignin.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 8 — Cell: The Unit of Life, Section 8.3
questions.ncert_line: Middle lamella = calcium pectate (pectin). It cements adjacent cells together. First layer formed at cell plate during cytokinesis.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following statements about RuBisCO is correct?

options.B: It has both carboxylase and oxygenase activity
options.C: It is found only in C4 plants

questions.explanation: Correct: B. RuBisCO has dual activity. Oxygenase activity causes photorespiration — wasteful process in C3 plants.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 13 — Photosynthesis in Higher Plants, Section 13.4
questions.ncert_line: RuBisCO (ribulose bisphosphate carboxylase/oxygenase) can both fix CO2 (carboxylase) and react with O2 (oxygenase → photorespiration).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which plant hormone promotes cell elongation and is responsible for apical dominance?
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Auxin = cell elongation + apical dominance. Cytokin promotes lateral bud growth (breaks apical dominance).
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 15 — Plant Growth and Development, Section 15.3
questions.ncert_line: Auxin (IAA) promotes cell elongation and causes apical dominance — apical bud suppresses lateral bud growth.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The edible part of conut (conut water + flesh) represents:
questions.explanation: Correct: A. Coconut water and flesh = endosperm. Endosperm provides nutrition for developing embryo.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 2 — Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants, Section 2.6
questions.ncert_line: Coconut water = free nuclear endosperm (liquid). Coconut flesh = cellular endosperm. Both are endosperm tissue.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The correct sequence of events in spermatogenesis is:
questions.explanation: Correct: A. Spermatogonia → Primary/Secondary spermatocyte → Spermatid → Sperm.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 3 — Human Reproduction, Section 3.2
questions.ncert_line: Spermatogenesis: Spermatogonia (2n) → Primary spermatocyte (2n) → Secondary spermatocyte (n) → Spermatid (n) → Sperm (n).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The Ti plasmid used in plant genetic engineering is obtained from:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ti plasmid from Agrobacterium tumefaciens. Used as natural genetic engineer of dicot plants.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 11 — Biotechnology: Principles and Processes, Section 11.4
questions.ncert_line: Ti plasmid (tumor-inducing) from Agrobacterium tumefaciens is a natural vector for introducing genes into dicot plants.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Golden Rice is genetically modified to produce:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Golden Rice produces beta-carotene. Addresses Vitamin A deficiency in developing countries.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 12 — Biotechnology and its Applications, Section 12.2
questions.ncert_line: Golden Rice was engineered to produce beta-carotene (provitamin A) in the endosperm to address Vitamin A deficiency.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: RNA interference (RNAi) is used in biotechnology to:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. RNAi = gene silencing using dsRNA. Used in nematode control in tobacco (Meloidogyne).
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 12 — Biotechnology and its Applications, Section 12.3
questions.ncert_line: RNAi uses double-stranded RNA (dsRNA) complementary to target mRNA → mRNA degradation → gene silencing.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Identify the statement that is NOT correct regarding antibody structure:

options.A: Constant region of heavy and light chains are located at C-terminus of antibody molecules
options.B: Each antibody has two light and two heavy chains
options.C: The heavy and light chains are held together by disulfide bonds
options.D: Antigen binding site is located at C-terminal region of antibody molecules

questions.explanation: Official NEET 2025 answer
questions.ncert_line: To be added — use this question as pattern template

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following does NOT correctly describe Mendel's Law of Independent Assortment?

options.A: It applies to genes located on different (non-homologous) chromosomes
options.B: Segregation of one pair of alles is independent of the segregation of another pair
options.C: It can generate new combinations of characters
options.D: It always applies even when two genes are on the same chromosome

questions.explanation: Correct answer is D. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 5 — Principles of Inheritance and Variation, Section 5.1.3: Law of Independent Assortment
questions.ncert_line: The Law of Independent Assortment states that when two pairs of traits are combined in a hybrid, the segregation of one pair of characters is independent of the other pair of characters.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which statement about ABO blood groups is INCORRECT?

options.A: IA and IB are codominant alles
options.B: A person with genotype IAIB has blood group AB
options.C: A person with genotype ii has blood group O
options.D: The IA alle is recessive to the i alle

questions.explanation: Correct answer is D. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 5 — Principles of Inheritance and Variation, Section 5.4: Codominance
questions.ncert_line: The three alles IA, IB and i control the ABO blood groups. IA and IB are codominant and both are dominant over i. A person with genotype ii has blood group O.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following statements about haemophilia is INCORRECT?

options.A: It is an X-linked recessive trait
options.C: Sons of a haemophilic father will always be haemophilic
options.D: Haemophilic daughters are born only when father is haemophilic AND mother is at least a carrier

questions.explanation: Correct answer is C. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 5 — Principles of Inheritance and Variation, Section 5.5: Sex-linked Inheritance
questions.ncert_line: The sons of a haemophilic father will not be haemophilic since they receive the Y chromosome from father and X chromosome from their mother.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In the lac operon, which of the following is INCORRECT?

options.A: The repressor protein is encoded by the regulator gene
options.B: In the absence of lactose, the repressor binds to the operator
options.C: Lactose acts as an inducer by binding to the repressor
options.D: When lactose binds to the operator, transcription proceeds

questions.explanation: Correct answer is D. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 6 — Molecular Basis of Inheritance, Section 6.7: Regulation of Gene Expression
questions.ncert_line: In the absence of inducer (lactose), the repressor binds to the operator and blocks transcription. When lactose (inducer) is present, it binds to the repressor, inactivating it, and transcription proceeds.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is INCORRECT about a typical angiosperm embryo sac at maturity?

options.B: It has 3 antipodal cells, 2 synergids, 1 egg cell, and 1 central cell
options.C: The central cell contains 2 polar nuclei
options.D: All 8 nuclei in the embryo sac are contained in 8 separate cells

questions.explanation: Correct answer is D. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 2 — Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants, Section 2.4.2: Female Gametophyte
questions.ncert_line: A typical angiosperm embryo sac at maturity has 7 cells with 8 nuclei — 3 antipodal cells, 2 synergids, 1 egg cell, and 1 large central cell with 2 polar nuclei.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is INCORRECT about oogenesis?

options.A: Primary oocyte is arrested at prophase I
options.B: Meiosis I is completed before ovulation
options.C: Meiosis II is completed after fertilisation
options.D: Meiosis II begins after fertilisation takes place

questions.explanation: Correct answer is D. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 3 — Human Reproduction, Section 3.2.3: Oogenesis
questions.ncert_line: Oogenesis begins during fetal development. The oogonia start meiosis but are arrested at prophase I (primary oocyte). Meiosis I completes just before ovulation. Meiosis II is completed only after fertilisation.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is INCORRECT about C4 plants?

options.A: The first stable product of CO₂ fixation is oxaloacetic acid (OAA)
options.B: CO₂ fixation first occurs in mesophyll cells
options.C: Rubisco is absent in C4 plants
options.D: The bundle sheath cells carry out the Calvin cycle

questions.explanation: Correct answer is C. NCERT Reference: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 13 — Photosynthesis in Higher Plants, Section 13.5.2: C4 Pathway
questions.ncert_line: In C3 plants, the first stable product of carbon fixation is 3-phosphoglyceric acid (3-PGA), a 3-carbon compound. In C4 plants, the first stable product is oxaloacetic acid (OAA), a 4-carbon compound, formed in mesophyll cells.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT correctly matched with its primary function?
questions.explanation: Correct answer is D. NCERT Reference: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 15 — Plant Growth and Development, Section 15.4: Plant Growth Regulators
questions.ncert_line: Cytokins promote cell division. Abscisic acid (ABA) promotes seed dormancy and stomatal closure. Ethylene is a gaseous plant hormone that promotes fruit ripening and abscission. Gibberellins promote stem elongation.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following statements about agarose gel electrophoresis is INCORRECT?

options.A: DNA fragments migrate toward the anode
options.B: Smaller fragments move faster and travel farther from the wells
options.C: DNA is stained with ethidium bromide and visualised under UV light
options.D: Larger DNA fragments are found closer to the positive electrode (anode)

questions.explanation: Correct answer is D. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 11 — Biotechnology: Principles and Processes, Section 11.2: Gel Electrophoresis
questions.ncert_line: In agarose gel electrophoresis, DNA fragments migrate toward the anode (positive electrode) since DNA is negatively charged. Smaller fragments migrate faster and are found farther from the wells. DNA is stained with ethidium bromide.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is INCORRECT about antibody structure?

options.A: Each antibody molecule has two light chains and two heavy chains
options.B: The heavy and light chains are held together by disulfide bonds
options.C: The antigen binding site is located at the C-terminal region of both chains
options.D: The constant region of the chains determines effector functions

questions.explanation: Correct answer is C. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 8 — Human Health and Disease, Section 8.2: Antibody
questions.ncert_line: In an antibody molecule, the antigen binding site is located at the N-terminal region of both heavy and light chains (variable region). The constant region determines effector function.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following statements about viruses is INCORRECT?

options.B: They may contain either DNA or RNA but never both
options.C: TMV was the first virus to be crystallised
options.D: All viruses have a protein coat and lipid envelope

questions.explanation: Correct: D. Ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 2 — Biological Classification, Section 2.6: Viruses
questions.ncert_line: Viruses are non-cellular enties that multiply only inside a living host cell. They have either DNA or RNA as genetic material, never both. They are acellular and obligate parasites. Tobacco Mosaic Virus (TMV) was the first virus to be crystallised by Wendell Stanley.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is INCORRECT about a dorsiventral (dicot) leaf?

options.A: Palisade mesophyll is present on the upper (adaxial) side
options.B: Stomata are more numerous on the lower (abaxial) surface
options.C: Spongy mesophyll has loosely arranged cells with air spaces
options.D: Stomata are equally distributed on both surfaces

questions.explanation: Correct: D. Ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 6 — Anatomy of Flowering Plants, Section 6.2.4: Anatomy of Leaf
questions.ncert_line: A dorsiventral (dicot) leaf has upper palisade mesophyll (compact, photosynthetically active) and lower spongy mesophyll (loosely arranged with air spaces). A dorsiventral leaf has more stomata on the lower (abaxial) surface.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is INCORRECT about the cockroach (Periplaneta americana)?

options.A: It has an open circulatory system
options.C: It has a closed tracheal system for respiration
options.D: The fat body is the main metabolic and storage organ

questions.explanation: Correct: C. Ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 7 — Structural Organisation in Animals, Section 7.3: Cockroach
questions.ncert_line: The cockroach (Periplaneta americana) has an open circulatory system. The fat body is the main metabolic organ. Excretion occurs via Malpighian tubules. Tracheal system is used for respiration. The compound eye can detect polarised light.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following statements about Bryophytes is INCORRECT?

options.A: They are called amphibians of the plant kingdom
options.D: The dominant phase is the gametophyte

questions.explanation: Correct: C. Bryophytes do NOT have vascular tissue — that is the key feature of pteridophytes onwards.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 3 — Plant Kingdom, Section 3.2
questions.ncert_line: Bryophytes lack vascular tissue (xylem and phloem). They are non-vascular plants.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Identify the set of correct statements: A. The flowers of Vallisneria are colourful and produce nectar; B. The flowers of waterlily are not pollinated by water; C. In most of water-pollinated species, the pollen grains are protected from wetting; D. Pollen grains of some hydrophytes are long and ribbon like; E. In some hydrophytes, the pollen grains are carried passively inside water.

options.A: A, C, D and E only
options.B: B, C, D and E only
options.D: A, B, C and D only

questions.explanation: Official NEET answer: B

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following statements is incorrect?

options.A: Bio-reactors are used to produce small scale bacterial cultures.
options.B: Bio-reactors have an agitator system, an oxygen delivery system and foam control system.
options.C: A bio-reactor provides optimal growth conditions for achieving the desired product.
options.D: Most commonly used bio-reactors are of stirring type.

questions.explanation: Official NEET answer: A

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Given below are two statements: Statement I: Gause's competive exclusive principle states that two closely related species competing for different resources cannot exist indefinitely. Statement II: According to Gause's principle, during competion, the inferior will be eliminated. This may be true if resources are limiting.

options.A: Statement I is true but Statement II is false.
options.B: Statement I is false but statement II is true.
options.C: Both statement I and Statement II are true.
options.D: Both statement I and Statement II are false.

questions.explanation: Official NEET answer: B

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: An electric lift with a maximum load of 2000 kg (lift + passengers) is moving up with a constant speed of 1.5 ms⁻¹ The frictional force opposing the motion is 3000 N. The minimum power delivered by the motor to the lift in watts is: (g=10 m s⁻²)
questions.explanation: Official NEET answer: D

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Copper crystallises in fcc unit cell with cell edge length of 3.608×10⁻⁸ cm. The density of copper is 8.92 g cm⁻³ Calculate the atomic mass of copper.
questions.explanation: Official NEET answer: B

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Identify the incorrect statement related to Pollination:
