Option D is wrong because the NCERT text states that MORE solar energy is available in the tropics, which contributes to higher productivity, not reduced competion.
questions.ncert_line: Tropical latitudes have remained relatively undisturbed for millions of years and thus, had a long evolutionary time for species diversification.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Lactic acid bacteria (LAB) improve the nutritional quality of curd by increasing which vitamin?
questions.explanation: NCERT Biology (Class 12, Chapter 10 - Microbes in Human Welfare) clearly states that Lactic Acid Bacteria (LAB) convert milk into curd and also improve its nutritional quality by increasing vitamin B12 content. Option A (Vitamin C) is an antioxidant vitamin not associated with LAB activity. Option B (Vitamin D) is a fat-soluble vitamin produced mainly through sunlight exposure. Option D (Vitamin A) is associated with vision and immunity, not LAB fermentation. Note: This topic is covered in Chapter 10 (Microbes in Human Welfare), not Chapter 8 (Human Health and Disease).
questions.ncert_ref: NCERT Class12 — Human Health and Disease
questions.ncert_line: converting milk to curd, which also improves its nutritional quality by increasing vitamin B12.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Streptokinase, used as a 'clot buster' to remove clots from blood vessels, is produced by which microorganism?
questions.explanation: NCERT clearly states that Streptokinase is produced by the bacterium Streptoccus and is used as a clot buster in heart attack patients. Option A (Clostridium butylicum) is a bacterium that produces butyric acid. Option C (Lactobacillus) is a bacterium that produces lactic acid. Option D (Acetobacter aceti) is a bacterium that produces acetic acid. None of these produce Streptokinase.
questions.ncert_ref: NCERT Class12 — Human Health and Disease
questions.ncert_line: Streptokinase produced by the bacterium Streptoccus and modified by genetic engineering is used as a 'clot buster' for removing clots from the blood vessels of patients who have undergone myocardial infarction leading to heart attack.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which cells in the seminiferous tubule provide nutrition to the male germ cells?
questions.explanation: NCERT clearly states that Sertoli cells provide nutrition to the germ cells inside the seminiferous tubule. Option A (Leydig cells) is wrong — Leydig cells are found in interstial spaces and synthesise androgens, not provide nutrition to germ cells. Option C (Spermatogonia) is wrong — spermatogonia are the male germ cells themselves that undergo meiotic divisions to form sperms. Option D (Interstial cells) is another name for Leydig cells and their function is hormone secretion, not nutrition. Note: This topic belongs to Chapter 'Human Reproduction' (Chapter 3 in Class 12 Biology), not 'Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants' (Chapter 2). The NCERT line cited is accurate.
questions.ncert_ref: NCERT Class12 — Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants
questions.ncert_line: Sertoli cells provide nutrition to the germ cells.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: What is the main function of the scrotum in males?

options.B: It secretes androgens for sperm maturation
options.C: It maintains a low temperature necessary for spermatogenesis
options.D: It produces seminal plasma to nourish sperms

questions.explanation: NCERT states the scrotum maintains a temperature 2–2.5°C lower than the normal body temperature, which is necessary for spermatogenesis. Option A is wrong — storage of sperms is the function of the epidymis and vas deferens, not the scrotum. Option B is wrong — androgens are secreted by Leydig cells inside the testis, not by the scrotum. Option D is wrong — seminal plasma is produced by accessory glands such as seminal vesicles, prostate and bulbourethral glands. Note: This question belongs to the chapter 'Human Reproduction' (Class 12 Biology), not 'Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants'.
questions.ncert_ref: NCERT Class12 — Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants
questions.ncert_line: The scrotum helps in maintaing the low temperature of the tes (2–2.5°C lower than the normal internal body temperature) necessary for spermatogenesis.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which component of seminal plasma is secreted by the bulbourethral glands?
questions.explanation: NCERT specifically states that secretions of the bulbourethral glands help in the lubrication of the penis. Option A is wrong — fructose is secreted by the seminal vesicles as an energy source for sperm. Option B is wrong — androgens are synthesised and secreted by Leydig cells in the interstial spaces of the testis, not by bulbourethral glands. Option C is wrong — calcium is mentioned as a component of seminal plasma overall, but it is not the specific secretion attributed to bulbourethral glands. Note: This question belongs to the chapter 'Human Reproduction' (Chapter 3 in Class 12 Biology), not 'Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants.'
questions.ncert_ref: NCERT Class12 — Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants
questions.ncert_line: The secretions of bulbourethral glands also helps in the lubrication of the penis.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: After the first meiotic division of a primary spermatocyte, the cells produced are called:
questions.explanation: After the first meiotic division (meiosis I), a primary spermatocyte forms two secondary spermatocytes. These secondary spermatocytes are haploid (23 chromosomes each). Option A (spermatids) are formed after the second meiotic division (meiosis II) of secondary spermatocytes. Option B (spermatogonia) are the original diploid germ cells that multiply by mitosis. Option D (spermatozoa) are the final mature sperms formed after spermiogenesis. Note: The NCERT line cited is from the chapter 'Human Reproduction' (Chapter 3 in Class 12 Biology), not 'Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants' (Chapter 2). The subject matter and NCERT line are otherwise accurate.
questions.ncert_ref: NCERT Class12 — Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants
questions.ncert_line: A primary spermatocyte completes the first meiotic division (reduction division) leading to formation of two equal, haploid cells called secondary spermatocytes, which have only 23 chromosomes each.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which portal system carries hypothalamic hormones to the anterior pituitary?
questions.explanation: NCERT clearly states that hypothalamic hormones reach the anterior pituitary through a portal circulatory system. The hypothalamus produces releasing and inhibiting hormones that travel through the hypothalamo-hypophyseal portal system to regulate anterior pituitary function. Option B is wrong because the lymphatic system carries lymph, not hypothalamic hormones to the pituitary. Option C is wrong because the coronary system supplies the heart muscle. Option D is wrong because the systemic venous system is not the route described for this specific transport.
questions.ncert_ref: NCERT Class11 — Chemical Coordination and Integration
questions.ncert_line: These hormones reach the pituitary gland through a portal circulatory system and regulate the functions of the anterior pituitary.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which hypothalamic hormone stimulates the pituitary to synthesise and release gonadotrophins?
questions.explanation: NCERT explicitly states that GnRH from the hypothalamus stimulates the pituitary to synthesise and release gonadotrophins. Option B is wrong because somatostatin is a hypothalamic hormone that inhibits the release of growth hormone — it does not stimulate gonadotrophin release. Option C is wrong because MSH is secreted by pars intermedia of the pituitary, not the hypothalamus, and it acts on melanocytes. Option D is wrong because prolactin is produced by the anterior pituitary (pars distalis), not the hypothalamus.
questions.ncert_ref: NCERT Class11 — Chemical Coordination and Integration
questions.ncert_line: A hypothalamic hormone called Gonadotrophin releasing hormone (GnRH) stimulates the pituitary synthesis and release of gonadotrophins.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Excess secretion of growth hormone in adults, especially in middle age, leads to severe disfigurement of the face. What is this condition called?
questions.explanation: Acromegaly is the correct term for severe facial disfigurement caused by excess GH in adults — directly stated in NCERT. Gigantism is caused by over-secretion of GH in growing children, not adults. Cretinism results from hypothyroidism during pregnancy affecting the baby. Pituitary dwarfism results from low secretion of GH, not excess.
questions.ncert_ref: NCERT Class11 — Chemical Coordination and Integration
questions.ncert_line: Excess secretion of growth hormone in adults especially in middle age can result in severe disfigurement (especially of the face) called Acromegaly.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which hormone secreted by the pineal gland helps in maintaing the normal sleep-wake cycle and body temperature rhythm?
questions.explanation: Melatonin is secreted by the pineal gland and regulates the 24-hour diurnal rhythm including the sleep-wake cycle — directly stated in NCERT. Oxytocin is released from the posterior pituitary and stimulates uterine contractions and milk ejection. Vasopressin (ADH) acts on the kidney to promote water resorption. Prolactin regulates growth of mammary glands and milk formation, not the sleep-wake cycle.
questions.ncert_ref: NCERT Class11 — Chemical Coordination and Integration
questions.ncert_line: Pineal secretes a hormone called melatonin. Melatonin plays a very important role in the regulation of a 24-hour (diurnal) rhythm of our body. For example, it helps in maintaing the normal rhythms of sleep-wake cycle, body temperature.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: What is the embryonic origin of muscle tissue in the human body?
questions.explanation: NCERT explicitly states that muscle is a specialised tissue of mesodermal origin. Option B is wrong because ectoderm gives rise to skin and nervous tissue, not muscles. Option C is wrong because endoderm gives rise to the ling of the gut and internal organs, not muscle tissue. Option D is wrong because neural crest cells give rise to certain head structures and pigment cells, not general muscle tissue.
questions.ncert_line: Muscle is a specialised tissue of mesodermal origin.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The sarcoplasmic reticulum in a muscle fibre acts as the storehouse of which ion?
questions.explanation: NCERT clearly states that the sarcoplasmic reticulum stores calcium ions. These calcium ions are later released into the sarcoplasm during muscle contraction. Option A (sodium ions) is wrong — sodium is mainly involved in generating action potentials across membranes, not stored in sarcoplasmic reticulum. Option B (potassium ions) is wrong — potassium is important in resting membrane potential but is not stored in sarcoplasmic reticulum. Option D (magnesium ions) is wrong — magnesium is a cofactor for some enzymes but the sarcoplasmic reticulum specifically stores calcium, not magnesium.
questions.ncert_line: The endoplasmic reticulum, i.e., sarcoplasmic reticulum of the muscle fibres is the store house of calcium ions.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: During muscle contraction, what happens when calcium ions bind to the troponin subunit on actin filaments?

options.A: ATP is directly synthesised at the actin filament
options.B: The masking of active sites for myosin on actin is removed
options.C: Tropomyosin permanently detaches from the actin filament
options.D: The Z line dissolves to allow filament sliding

questions.explanation: NCERT states that calcium binding to troponin removes the masking of active sites for myosin on actin filaments, allowing myosin heads to bind and form cross bridges. Option A is wrong — ATP is not synthesised at actin filaments; ATP hydrolysis provides energy for contraction. Option C is wrong — NCERT does not say tropomyosin permanently detaches; it says the active sites are unmasked by troponin's change. Option D is wrong — the Z line does not dissolve; it is the structural anchor for thin filaments and remains intact during contraction.
questions.ncert_line: Increase in Ca++ level leads to the binding of calcium with a subunit of troponin on actin filaments and thereby remove the masking of active sites for myosin.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In algae, asexual reproduction most commonly involves the production of:
questions.explanation: NCERT clearly states that the most common spores produced during asexual reproduction in algae are zoospores, which are flagellated (motile) and germinate into new plants. Pollen grains are wrong because they are found in seed plants, not in algae. Conidia are wrong because they are asexual spores produced by fungi, not algae. Aplanospores are non-motile spores and are not described in NCERT as the most common type in algae.
questions.ncert_line: Asexual reproduction is by the production of different types of spores, the most common being the zoospores. They are flagellated (motile) and on germination gives rise to new plants.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Chlorella is used as a food supplement by space travellers. What makes Chlorella suitable for this purpose?

options.A: It is a unicellular alga rich in proteins
options.B: It is a multicellular alga rich in carbohydrates
options.C: It produces large amounts of hydrocolloids
options.D: It is a filamentous alga rich in lipids

questions.explanation: The NCERT text clearly states that Chlorella is a unicellular alga rich in proteins, which makes it a suitable food supplement for space travellers. Option B is wrong because Chlorella is unicellular, not multicellular, and it is rich in proteins, not carbohydrates. Option C is wrong because hydrocolloid production is associated with marine brown and red algae, not Chlorella. Option D is wrong because Chlorella is unicellular, not filamentous, and it is known for proteins, not lipids.
questions.ncert_line: Chlorella, a unicellular alga rich in proteins, is used as food supplement even by space travellers.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The colour of brown algae ranges from olive green to various shades of brown. Which pigment is directly responsible for this variation in colour?
questions.explanation: NCERT directly states that the colour variation in brown algae depends on the amount of the xanthophyll pigment fucoxanthin. Option B is wrong because r-phycoerythrin is the red pigment found in red algae (Rhodophyceae), not brown algae. Option C is wrong because chlorophyll b is a pigment found in green algae (Chlorophyceae) and is not responsible for brown colouration. Option D is wrong because chlorophyll d is listed as a pigment of red algae (Rhodophyceae), not brown algae.
questions.ncert_line: They vary in colour from olive green to various shades of brown depending upon the amount of the xanthophyll pigment, fucoxanthin present in them.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Gause's principle of competive exclusion states that:

options.A: More abundant species will exclude the less abundant species through competion.
options.B: Competion for the same resources excludes species having different food preferences.
options.C: No two species can occupy the same niche indefinitely for the same limiting resources.
options.D: Larger organisms exclude smaller ones through competion.

questions.explanation: This is a direct fact from NCERT syllabus.

questions.explanation: This is a direct fact from NCERT syllabus.

questions.question: In context of Amniocentesis, which of the following statement is incorrect ?

options.A: It is usually done when a woman is between 14-16 weeks pregnant.
options.B: It is used for prenatal sex determination

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Identify A, B and C in the diagrammatic representation of the mechanism of hormone action.

options.A: A= Protein Hormone; B= Cyclic AMP; c= Hormone-receptor Complex
options.B: A= Steroid Hormone; B= Hormone-receptor Complex; c= Protein
options.C: A= Protein Hormone; B= Receptor C Cyclic AMP
options.D: A= Steroid Hormone; B= Receptor; c= Second Messenger

questions.explanation: 'A' is a hormone such as FSH that cannot cross lipid bilayer therefore it interacts with the membrane-bound receptors and does not enter the target cell, but generates second messengers. Peptide, polypeptide, protein hormones and catecholamines act through this mechanism. 'B' represents the extracellular receptors present on cell surface that forms a hormone-receptor complex which brings about conformational changes in the cytoplasmic part of the receptor. This cytoplasmic part can produces second messengers such as Ca+2, cAMP, IP3 etc. which activates the existing enzyme system of the cell and accelerates the biochemical reactions in the cell. 'C' represents the second messenger.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Humans have acquired immune system that produces antibodies to neutralize pathogens. Still innate immune system is present at the time of birth because it

options.B: is very specific and uses different macrophages.
options.C: produces memory cells for mounting fast secondary response.
options.D: has natural killer cells which can phagocytose and destroy microbes

questions.explanation: Innate immunity is non-specific and is accomplished by providing different types of barriers to the entry of foreign agent or any pathogen into our body. Passive immunity is specific and is a branch of humoral immunity. Natural killer cells are a type of lymphocytes which will produce proteins called perforins that create pores on plasma membrane of tumor cells and virally infected cells through which ECF enters and kill the cells. Innate immunity does not produce memory cells hence, no secondary immune response is seen.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Select the correct sequence of events.

options.A: Gametogenesis → Gamete transfer → Syngamy → Zygote → Cell division (Cleavage) → Cell differentiation → Organogenesis.
options.B: Gametogenesis → Gamete transfer → Syngamy → Zygote → Cell division (Cleavage) → Cell differentiation → Organogenesis.
options.C: Gametogenesis → Gamete transfer → Syngamy → Zygote → Cell division (Cleavage) → Organogenesis → Cell differentiation.
options.D: Gametogenesis → Syngamy → Gamete transfer → Zygote → Cell division (Cleavage) → Cell differentiation → Organogenesis.

questions.explanation: Gametogenesis → Formation of Gametes Gamete transfer → Insemination Syngamy → Fusion of gametes and leading to formation of gametes Zygote Cell division (Cleavage) → Repeated cell division. Cell differentiation → Specialization of cell to perform specific function. Organogenesis → Formation of organ

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following hormones is responsible for both the milk ejection reflex and the foetal ejection reflex?
questions.explanation: Foetal ejection reflex triggers release of oxytocin from the maternal anterior pituitary gland. Oxytocin acts on the uterine muscles and causes stronger uterine contractions. Milk ejection reflex is caused by combined neurogenic and hormonal reflexes that involve release of oxytocin from posterior pituitary gland.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: No new follicles develop in the luteal phase of the menstrual cycle because:

options.A: Both FSH and LH levels are low in the luteal phase.
options.B: Follicles do not remain in the ovary after ovulation.
options.C: FSH levels are high in the luteal phase.
options.D: LH levels are high in the luteal phase.

questions.explanation: Ovulation is followed by the luteal phase during which the corpus luteum secretes estrogen and progesterone that exerts negative feedback on hypothalamus which in turn inhibits the release of FSH and LH. This prevents the development of new follicles during the luteal phase.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In Australia, marsupials and placental mammals have evolved to share many similar characteristics. This type of evolution may be referred to as
questions.explanation: In Australia, marsupials and placental mammals have evolved to share many similar characteristics. This type of evolution may be referred to as convergent evolution. Many placental mammals resemble the marsupial mammals not only in structure but also in leading similar ways of life. These similarities in these two different types of mammals (Marsupials and placentals) w.r.t. ancestors are due to living in the similar ecological niches. Hence, when two different types of organisms converge towards functional similarity one can conclude that convergent evolution has occurred.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: What will be the sequence of mRNA produced by the following stretch of DNA? 3′ ATGCATG 5′ TEMPLATE STRAND 5′ TACGTAC 3′ CODING STRAND
questions.explanation: 5′ TACGTAC 3′ (Coding strand) 5′ UACGUAC 3′ (m-RNA) In m-RNA at the place of thymine in coding strand uracil remain present.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following diseases is an autoimmune disorder?
questions.explanation: Myasthenia gravis is autoimmune disorder that affects neuromuscular junction leading to fatigue, weakening and paralysis of skeletal muscles.. Arthritis is inflammation of joints. Gout is inflammation of joints due to accumulation of uric acid crystals. • Osteoporosis is age related disorder characterised by decreased bone mass and increased chances of fractures.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Artificial light, extended work-time and reduced sleep-time disrupt the activity of -
questions.explanation: Artificial light, extended work time and reduced sleep-time disrupt the activity of pineal gland. Melatonin hormone of pineal gland plays a very important role in the regulation of a 24-hours (diurnal) rhythm of our body. Melatonin helps in maintaing the normal rhythms of sleep-wake cycle and body temperature.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following conditions will stimulate parathyroid gland to release parathyroid hormone ?

options.B: Fall in active Vitamin D levels

questions.explanation: Parathyroid hormone (PTH) is a hypercalcemic hormone. i.e. it increases the blood Ca2+ level. PTH acts on bones and stimulates the process of bone resorption (dissolution/demineralisation). PTH also stimulates reabsorption of Ca2+ by the renal tubules and increases Ca2+ absorption from the digested food.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following sexually transmitted diseases do not specifically affect reproductive organs ?
questions.explanation: AIDS and Hepatis B are sexually transmitted infections. However, they do not specifically affect the reproductive organs only. AIDS is HIV infection affecting the immune system and opportunistic pathogens can infect various body organs. Hepatis B affects liver. Agents causing genital herpes, genital warts and chlamydiasis produce lesions on genital organs and also directly affect the reproductive system.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The maximum volume of air a person can breathe in after a forced expiration is known as:
questions.explanation: Maximum amount of air expired by a person after forceful inspiration or inspired after forceful expiration is known as vital capacity 4600 ml Total amount of air in lungs at end of forceful inspiration is total lung capacity 5800 ml

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: All the components of the nodal tissue are autoexcitable. Why does the SA node act as the normal pacemaker?

options.A: SA node has the highest rate of depolarisation.
options.B: SA node has the lowest rate of depolarisation.
options.C: SA node is the only component to generate the threshold potential.
options.D: Only SA node can convey the action potential to the other components.

questions.explanation: SA node acts as pace-maker of heart because its autoexcitable tissue generates heart impulses at highest rate (frequency).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: A specialised nodal tissue embedded in the lower corner of the right atrium, close to Atrio-ventricular septum, delays the spreading of impulses to heart apex for about 0.1 sec. This delay allows
questions.explanation: The delay in transmission of impulse from SAN to the ventricles provided by AVN prevents simultaneous contraction of ventricles and auricles. This allows atria to empty completely before ventricles start contraction.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following receptors are specifically responsible for maintenance of balance of body and posture?

options.C: Hair cells and organ of corti

questions.explanation: Crista ampullaris Maintains the dynamic balance of the body Otolith organ →→ Consists of two structures i.e. saccule and utricle → The projecting ridge of the saccule and utricle is called macula. The otolith organ maintains the static balance of the body

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following cell organelles is present in the highest number in secretory cells?
questions.explanation: The important function of Golgi apparatus is to process, package and transport the materials for secretion. Therefore secretory cells have Golgi apparatus in highest number.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Non-membranous nucleoplasmic structures in nucleus are the site for active synthesis of
questions.explanation: Nucleolus is non-membranous nucleoplasmic structure in nucleus. Nucleolus is the site of ribosomal RNA (rRNA) synthesis.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following nucleic acids is present in an organism having 70 S ribosomes only?

options.A: Double stranded circular DNA with histone proteins
options.B: Single stranded DNA with protein coat
options.D: Double stranded DNA enclosed in nuclear membrane

questions.explanation: The organisms which have ribosomes of 70 S type are prokaryotes. Prokaryotes have double stranded DNA which is not enclosed in membrane.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: After meiosis I, the resultant daughter cells have

options.A: four times the amount of DNA in comparison to haploid gamete.
options.B: same amount of DNA as in the parent cell in S phase.
options.C: twice the amount of DNA in comparison to haploid gamete.
options.D: same amount of DNA in comparison to haploid gamete.

questions.explanation: Meiosis I is reductional division thus the resultant cell just after meiosis I will have half the number of chromosomes as compared to diploid parent cells. Therefore if the parent cell has 4C amount of DNA the daughter cell will have 2C amount of DNA and each gamete will have 1C amount of DNA.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following organic compounds is the main constituent of Lecithin?
questions.explanation: Lecithin is an example of phospholipids found in cell membrane. Casein is a phosphoprotein found in milk

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The main difference between active and passive transport across cell membrane is:

options.A: active transport occurs more rapidly than passive transport.
options.B: passive transport is non-selective whereas active transport is selective.
options.C: passive transport requires a concentration gradient across a biological membrane whereas active transport requires energy to move solutes.
options.D: passive transport is confined to anionic carrier proteins whereas active transport is confined to cationic channel proteins.

questions.explanation: In active transport, materials are transported across a membrane with the help of mobile carrier protein and ATP. In passive transport, substances move along the concentration gradient, i.e., from its higher concentration to its lower concentration.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Kwashiorkor disease is due to -

options.A: protein deficiency not accompanied by calorie deficiency
options.B: simultaneous deficiency of proteins and fats
options.C: simultaneous deficiency of proteins and calories

questions.explanation: Kwashiorkor is produced by protein deficiency but unaccompanied by calorie deficiency. It results from the replacement of mother's milk by a high calorie-low protein diet in a child more than one year in age.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following animals are true coelomates with bilateral symmetry?
questions.explanation: Annelids exhibit bilateral symmetry with metameric segmentation where external segments correspond to internal segments. Aschelminthes are pseudocoelomates and platyhelminthes are acoelomates. Adult echinoderms are bilaterally symmetrical.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The contrasting characteristics generally in a pair used for identification of animals in Taxonomic Key are referred to as:
questions.explanation: The keys are based on the set of contrasting characters in pair known as couplet.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Between which among the following, the relationship is not an example of commensalism?

options.B: Orchid and the tree on which it grows

questions.explanation: Female wasp and fig species show mutualism. While orchid on trees on which it grows, cattle egret and grazing cattle, and sea anemone and clown fish show commensalism.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: If an agricultural field is liberally irrigated for a prolonged period of time, it is likely to face a problem of:
questions.explanation: Irrigation of agricultural field for a prolonged time without proper drainage leads to waterlogging in the soil. Waterlogging draws salt to the surface of the soil and causes salinity.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following statements about methanogens is not correct?

options.B: They can be used to produce biogas
options.C: They are found in the rumen of cattle and their excreta
options.D: They grow aerobically and breakdown cellulose-rich food

questions.explanation: Methanogens are anaerobic chemoautotrophs. They grow in anaerobic condition and breakdown cellulose rich food.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In mung bean, resistance to yellow mosaic, virus and powdery mildew were brought about by:
questions.explanation: In mung bean, resistance to yellow mosaic virus and powdery mildew were induced by mutations.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Coca alkaloid or cocaine is obtained from:
questions.explanation: Coca alkaloids or cocaine is obtained from, Erythroxylum coca, a native to South America.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Among the following pairs of microbes, which pair has both the microbes that can be used as biofertilizers?
questions.explanation: Cyanobacteria like Anabaena, Nostoc etc. and Rhizobium are able to fix atmospheric nitrogen and used as biofertilizers. Rhizopus and Aspergillus are used in production of other compounds of human benefit.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Given below are four statements pertaing to separation of DNA fragments using Gel electrophoresis. Identify the incorrect statements. (a) DNA is negatively charged molecule and so it is loaded on gel towards the Anode terminal. (b) DNA fragments travel along the surface of the gel whose concentration does not affect movement of DNA. (c) Smaller the size of DNA fragment, larger is the distance it travels through it. (d) Pure DNA can be visualized directly by exposing to UV radiation.
questions.explanation: DNA fragments are negatively charged molecules. Separated by forcing them to move towards positive electrode i.e. anode. Increasing the concentration of a gel reduces the migration speed of DNA. Smaller the fragment size, farther it moves from the point of loading i.e. the well. DNA fragment can only be visualised under UV light after staing with ethidium bromide and not directly.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: An enzyme catalysing the removal of nucleotides from ends of DNA is :
questions.explanation: Restriction enzymes belong to a larger class of enzymes called Nucleases. Nucleases are of two different kinds: (1) Exonucleases which remove nucleotides from the end of a DNA molecule (2) Endonucleases, which break internal phosphodiester bonds at palindromic sites that are highly specific.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In RNAi, the genes are silenced using:
questions.explanation: RNAi involves silencing of a specific mRNA and therefore the expression of a gene by formation of a dsRNA molecule. The dsRNA which is formed by binding of a complementary RNA (anti-sense RNA) molecule to original mRNA thereby preventing translation of the original mRNA.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which is the most common type of embryo sac in angiosperms?

options.A: Bisporic with two sequential mitotic divisions
options.B: Tetrasporic with one mitotic stage of divisions
options.C: Monosporic with three sequential mitotic divisions
options.D: Monosporic with two sequential mitotic divisions

questions.explanation: Monosporic embryo sac is the most common type of embryo sac in flowering plants which develops after one meiosis and three sequential mitosis in megaspore mother cell.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: From the following, identify the correct combination of salient features of Genetic Code -
questions.explanation: Genetic code is universal, unambiguous, non overlapping and degenerate in nature.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which scientist experimentally proved that DNA is the sole genetic material in bacteriophage?
questions.explanation: A Hershey and M Chase using radiolabelled sulphur and phosphorus in bacteriophage, proved that DNA is genetic material.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In the process of transcription in Eukaryotes, the RNA polymerase I transcribes -

options.B: mRNA with additional processing, capping and tailing

questions.explanation: During transcription in eukaryotes, RNA polymerase I transcribes 5.8S, 18S and 28S rRNA.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In which genetic condition, each cell in the affected person, has three sex chromosomes XXY?
questions.explanation: Klinefelter's syndrome is caused due to presence of an additional copy of X-chromosome resulting into 44+XXY type chromosome complement.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following statements is correct about the origin and evolution of men?

options.A: Neanderthal men lived in Asia between 1,000 and 40,000 years back.
options.C: The Dryopithecus and Ramapithecus primates existing 15 million years ago, walked like men.

questions.explanation: Agriculture came around 10,000 years back and human settlements started. Homo habilis probably did not eat meat. The Dryopithecus and Ramapithecus primates existing 15 mya. They are hairy and walked like gorillas and chimpanzees.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The production of gametes by the parents, the formation of zygotes, the F1 and F2 plants, can be understood using
questions.explanation: With the help of Punnett square the production of gametes, the formation of zygotes and the F1 and F2 plants can be understood.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In Hatch and Slack pathway, the primary CO₂ acceptor is
questions.explanation: PEP (Phosphoenol pyruvate) is the primary CO2 acceptor in C4 or Hatch and Slack pathway.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Removal of shoot tips is a very useful technique to boost the production of tea-leaves. This is because :

options.B: Gibberellins prevent bolting and are inactivated.
options.C: Auxins prevent leaf drop at early stages.
options.D: Effect of auxins is removed and growth of lateral buds is enhanced.

questions.explanation: Auxin shows apical dominance. Removal of auxin by removal of shoot tips prevents the apical dominance and promotes the growth of lateral buds which is a very useful in tea leaves production.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: One scientist cultured Cladophora in a suspension of Azotobacter and illuminated the culture by splitting light through a prism. He observed that bacteria accumulated mainly in the region of:
questions.explanation: Azotobacter is aerobic bacteria. Cladophora is green alga. T.W. Engelmann split light into its spectral components and detected that aerobic bacteria accumulated mainly in the region of blue and red light of the split spectrum.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In order to increase the yield of sugarcane crop, which of the following plant growth regulators should be sprayed?
questions.explanation: Gibberellins shows bolting when gibberellin is on sugarcane crop, the length of the stem increases.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: What type of pollination takes place in Vallisneria?

options.A: Male flowers are carried by water currents to female flowers at surface of water.
options.B: Pollination occurs in submerged condition by water.
options.C: Flowers emerge above surface of water and pollination occurs by insects.
options.D: Flowers emerge above water surface and pollen is carried by wind.

questions.explanation: Vallisneria shows epihydrophily. Male flowers after breakage float on the surface of water and reach the female flowers.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In which one of the following, both autogamy and geitonogamy are prevented?
questions.explanation: Papaya is a dioecious plant in which male and female flowers are produced on two separate plants. Hence it prevents both autogamy as well as geitonogamy. Castor and Maize: Monoecious and unisexual Wheat: Monoecious and bisexual.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Prosthetic groups differ from co-enzymes in that-

options.A: They can serve as co-factors in a number of enzyme catalyzed reactions
options.B: They require metal ions for their activity
options.C: They (prosthetic groups) are tightly bound to apoenzymes
options.D: Their association with apoenzymes is transient

questions.explanation: Prosthetic groups are organic compounds that are tightly bound to the apoenzyme but coenzymes are associated with the apoenzyme last for a short period of time i.e. transiently.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Crossing over takes place between which chromatids and in which stage of the cell cycle?

options.A: Non-sister chromatids of non-homologous chromosomes at Pachytene stage of prophase I
options.B: Non-sister chromatids of non-homologous chromosomes at Zygotene stage of prophase I
options.C: Non-sister chromatids of homologous chromosomes at Pachytene stage of prophase I
options.D: Non-sister chromatids of homologous chromosomes at Zygotene stage of prophase I

questions.explanation: Crossing over takes place between non-sister chromatids of homologous chromosomes at pachytene stage.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: "Ramachandran plot" is used to confirm the structure of
questions.explanation: "Ramachandran plot" is used to confirm structure of proteins. A Ramachandran plot, is a way to visualize energetically allowed regions for backbone dihedral angles against of amino acid residues to protein structure.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is not a feature of active transport of solutes in plants ?
questions.explanation: Active transport is uphill transport which requires membrane proteins and ATP. It is highly selective.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following bacteria reduce nitrate in soil into nitrogen?
questions.explanation: Denitrification (reducing nitrates into nitrogen) is performed by Pseudomonas denitrificans & Thiobacillus denitrificans.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: What will be the direction of flow of water when a plant cell is placed in a hypotonic solution?

options.A: No flow of water in any direction
options.B: Water will flow in both directions
options.C: Water will flow out of the cell
options.D: Water will flow into the cell

questions.explanation: When a plant cell is placed in hypotonic solution water will flow into the cell as water moves from high water potential to low water potential.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Where is the respiratory electron transport system (ETS) located in plants?
questions.explanation: Electron transport system is located in inner mitochondrial membrane.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is against the rules of ICBN?

options.A: Generic and specific names should be written starting with small letters.
options.B: Hand written scientific names should be underlined.
options.C: Every species should have a generic name and a specific epithet.
options.D: Scientific names are in Latin and should be italized.

questions.explanation: The first word denoting the genus starts with a capital letter while the specific epithet starts with a small letter.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Mad cow disease in cattle is caused by an organism which has :
questions.explanation: Mad cow disease in cattle is caused by prions. Prions are disease causing agents having abnormally folded proteins.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In the dicot root the vascular cambium originates from:

options.A: Intrafascicular and interfascicular tissue in a ring
options.B: Tissue located below the phloem bundles and a portion of pericycle tissue above protoxylem.

questions.explanation: Vascular cambium is a secondary meristematic tissue, in dicot roots. It is originated from tissue located below the phloem bundles and a portion of pericycle tissue above the protoxylem.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following shows whorled phyllotaxy?
questions.explanation: In whorled phyllotaxy, more than two leaves arise at each node and form a whorl or a circle. Alstonia shows whorled phyllotaxy.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Regeneration of damaged growing grass following grazing is largely due to :
questions.explanation: Intercalary meristems are found in grasses, where they help to regenerate the parts removed by the grazing herbivores.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Bicarpellary ovary with obliquely placed septum is seen in :
questions.explanation: Bicarpellary ovary with obliquely placed septum is seen in the members of family Solanaceae eg. Solanum.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Select the incorrect statement regarding inbreeding.

options.A: Inbreeding depression cannot be overcome by out-crossing
options.B: Inbreeding helps in elimination of deleterious alles from the population
options.C: Inbreeding is necessary to evolve a pure line in any animal
options.D: Continued inbreeding reduces fertility and leads to inbreeding depression

questions.explanation: A single outcross often helps to overcome inbreeding depression. Inbreeding increases homozygosity and close inbreeding usually reduces fertility and even productivity causing inbreeding depression.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: A biocontrol agent to be a part of an integrated pest management should be

options.A: Species-specific and inactive on non-target organisms

questions.explanation: A good biocontrol agent should be species-specific and inactive on non-target organisms.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The two antibiotic resistance genes on pBR322 are for
questions.explanation: E.Coli cloning vector pBR322 contains antibiotic resistance genes for Ampicillin and Tetracycline.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Exploitation of bioresources of a nation by multinational companies without authorization from the concerned country is referred to as
questions.explanation: Biopiracy is the term used to refer to the use of bio-resources by multinational companies and other organisations without proper authorisation from the countries and people concerned without compensatory payment.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Carnivorous animals lions and leopards, occupy the same niche but lions predate mostly larger animals and leopards take smaller ones. This mechanism of competion is referred to as-
questions.explanation: If two species compete for the same resource, they could avoid competion by choosing, for instance different times for feeding or different foraging patterns. Such mechanism is called resource partioning.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Decline in the population of Indian native fishes due to introduction of Clarias gariepinus in river Yamuna can be categorised as
questions.explanation: Introduction of the African catfish Clarias gariepinus for aquaculture purposes has become a threat to indigenous catfishes in our rivers. Clarias gariepinus is an alien species for the communities in Yamuna.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In a marriage between male with blood group A and female with blood group B, the progeny had either blood group AB or B. What could be the possible genotype of parents?
questions.explanation: Male with blood group A can have IAi or IA. Female with blood group B can have genotype IB or IBi. For progeny to have blood group AB and B, the combination must be Male (IAi) and Female (IB).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which one of the following plants shows a very close relationship with a species of moth, where none of the two can complete its life cycle without the other?
questions.explanation: Sol. Yucca have an obligate mutualism with a species of moth i.e. Pronuba.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Pollen grains can be stored for several years in liquid nitrogen having a temperature of
questions.explanation: Sol. Pollen grains can be stored for several years in liquid nitrogen at -196°C (Cryopreservation)

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following elements is responsible for maintaing turgor in cells?
questions.explanation: Sol. Potassium helps in maintaing turgidity of cells.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: What is the role of NAD+ in cellular respiration?

options.A: It is a nucleotide source for ATP synthesis.
options.B: It functions as an electron carrier.
options.D: It is the final electron acceptor for anaerobic respiration.

questions.explanation: Sol. In cellular respiration, NAD+ act as an electron carrier.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is commonly used as a vector for introducing a DNA fragment in human lymphocytes?
questions.explanation: Sol. Retrovirus is commonly used as vector for introducing a DNA fragment in human lymphocyte. Gene therapy : Lymphocyte from blood of patient are grown in culture outside the body, a functional gene is introduced by using a retroviral vector, into these lymphocyte.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Use of bioresources by multinational companies and organisations without authorisation from the concerned country and its people is called
questions.explanation: Sol. Biopiracy is term used for or refer to the use of bioresources by multinational companies and other organisation without proper authorisation from the countries and people concerned with compensatory payment (definition of biopiracy given in NCERT).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In India, the organisation responsible for assessing the safety of introducing genetically modified organisms for public use is

options.B: Council for Scientific and Industrial Research (CSIR)

questions.explanation: Sol. Indian Government has setup organisation such as GEAC (Genetic Engineering Appraisal Committee) which will make decisions regarding the validity of GM research and safety of introducing GM- organism for public services. (Direct from NCERT).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The correct order of steps in Polymerase Chain Reaction (PCR) is
questions.explanation: Sol. This technique is used for making multiple copies of gene (or DNA) of interest in vitro. Each cycle has three steps (i) Denaturation (ii) Primer annealing (iii) Extension of primer

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: A 'new' variety of rice was patented by a foreign company, though such varieties have been present in India for a long time. This is related to
questions.explanation: Sol. In 1997, an American company got patent rights on Basmati rice through the US patent and trademark office that was actually been derived from Indian farmer's varieties. The diversity of rice in India is one of the richest in the world, 27 documented varieties of Basmati are grown in India. Indian basmati was crossed with semi- dwarf varieties and claimed as an invention or a novelty. Sharbati Sonora and Lerma Rojo are varieties of wheat.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following pairs is wrongly matched?

options.C: Starch synthesis in pea : Multiple alles

questions.explanation: Sol. Starch synthesis in pea is controlled by pleiotropic gene. Other options (1, 2 & 4) are correctly matched.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The experimental proof for semiconservative replication of DNA was first shown in a
questions.explanation: Sol. Semi- conservative DNA replication was first shown in Bacterium Escherichia coli by Matthew Meselson and Franklin Stahl.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following flowers only once in its life-time?
questions.explanation: Sol. Bamboo species are monocarpic i.e., flower generally only once in its life- time after 50-100 years. Jackfruit, paya and mango are polycarpic i.e., produce flowers and fruits many times in their life- time.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Offsets are produced by
questions.explanation: Sol. Offset is a vegetative part of a plant, formed by mitosis. Meiotic divisions do not occur in somatic cells. Parthenogenesis is the formation of embryo from ovum or egg without fertilisation. Parthenocarpy is the fruit formed without fertilisation, (generally seedless)

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following has proved helpful in preserving pollen as fossils?
questions.explanation: Sol. Sporopollenin cannot be degraded by enzyme; strong acids and alkali, therefore it is helpful in preserving pollen as fossil. Pollenkitt - Help in insect pollination. Cellulosic Intine - Inner sporoderm layer of pollen grain known as intine made up cellulose & pectin. Oil content - No role is pollen preservation.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Natality refers to

options.A: Number of individuals leaving the habitat
options.D: Number of individuals entering a habitat

questions.explanation: Sol. Natality refers to birth rate. Death rate - Mortality Number of individuals entering a habitat is Immigration Number of individuals leaving a habitat - Emigration

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: World Ozone Day is celebrated on
questions.explanation: Sol. World Ozone day is celebrated on 16th September. 5th June - World Environment Day 21st April - National Yellow Bat Day 22nd April - National Earth Day

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is a secondary pollutant?
questions.explanation: Sol. O3 (ozone) is a secondary pollutant. These are formed by the reaction of primary pollutant. CO - Quantitative pollutant CO2 - Primary pollutant SO2 - Primary pollutant

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: What type of ecological pyramid would be obtained with the following data? Secondary consumer : 120 g Primary consumer : 60 g Primary producer : 10 g
questions.explanation: Sol. The given data depicts the inverted pyramid of biomass, usually found in aquatic ecosystem. Pyramid of energy is always upright. Upright pyramid of biomass and numbers are not possible, as the data depicts primary producer is less than primary consumer and this is less than secondary consumers.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In stratosphere, which of the following elements acts as a catalyst in degradation of ozone and release of molecular oxygen?
questions.explanation: Sol. UV rays act on CFCs, releasing Cl atoms, chlorine reacts with ozone in sequential method converting into oxygen. Carbon, oxygen and Fe are not related to ozone layer depletion.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The two functional groups characteristic of sugars are
questions.explanation: Sol. Sugar is a common term used to denote carbohydrate. Carbohydrates are polyhydroxy aldehyde, ketone or their derivatives, which means they have carbonyl and hydroxyl groups.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which among the following is not a prokaryote?
questions.explanation: Sol. Saccharomyces i.e. yeast is an eukaryotic (unicellular fungi) Mycobacterium - a bacterium Oscillatoria and Nostoc are cyanobacteria.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The Golgi complex participates in
questions.explanation: Sol. Golgi complex, after processing releases secretory vesicles from their trans- face.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is not a product of light reaction of photosynthesis?
questions.explanation: Sol. ATP, NADPH and oxygen are products of light reaction, while NADH is a product of respiration process.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is true for nucleolus?

options.A: It takes part in spindle formation
options.C: Larger nucleoli are present in dividing cells
options.D: It is a site for active ribosomal RNA synthesis

questions.explanation: Sol. Nucleolus is a non membranous structure and is a site of r- RNA synthesis.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Stomatal movement is not affected by
questions.explanation: Sol. Light, temperature and concentration of CO2 affect opening and closing of stomata while they are not affected by O2 concentration.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The stage during which separation of the paired homologous chromosomes begins is
questions.explanation: Sol. Synaptonemal complex disintegrates. Terminalisation begins at diplotene stage i.e. chiasmata start to shift towards end.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Stomata in grass leaf are
questions.explanation: Sol. Grass being a monocot, has Dumb- bell shaped stomata in their leaves.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Secondary xylem and phloem in dicot stem are produced by
questions.explanation: Sol. Vascular cambium is partially secondary. Forms secondary xylem towards its inside and secondary phloem towards outsides. 4-10 times more secondary xylem is produced than secondary phloem.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Pneumatophores occur in
questions.explanation: Sol. Halophytes like mangrooves have pneumatophores. Apogeotropic (-vely geotropic) roots having lenticels called pneumatodes to uptake O2.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Casparian strips occur in
questions.explanation: Sol. Endodermis have casparian strip on radial and inner tangential wall. It is suberin rich.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Plants having little or no secondary growth are
questions.explanation: Sol. Grasses are monocots and monocots usually do not have secondary growth. Palm like monocots have anomalous secondary growth.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Sweet potato is a modified
questions.explanation: Sol. Sweet potato is a modified adventious root for storage of food. Rhizomes are underground modified stem. Tap root is primary root directly elongated from the radicle.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Select the wrong statement:

options.A: Pseudopodia are locomotory and feeding structures in Sporozoans
options.C: Cell wall is present in members of Fungi and Plantae
options.D: Mitochondria are the powerhouse of the cell in all kingdoms except Monera

questions.explanation: Sol. Pseudopodia are locomotory structures in sarcodines (Amoeboid)

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: After karyogamy followed by meiosis, spores are produced exogenously in
questions.explanation: Sol. In Agaricus (a genus of basidiomycetes), basidiospores or meiospores are produced exogenously. Neurospora (a genus of ascomycetes) produces ascospores as meiospores but endogenously inside the ascus. Alternaria (a genus of deuteromycetes) does not produce sexual spores. Saccharomyces (Unicellular ascomycetes) produces ascospores, endogenously.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Winged pollen grains are present in
questions.explanation: Sol. In Pinus, winged pollen grains are present. It is extended outer exine on two lateral sides to form the wings of pollen. It is the characteristic feature, only in Pinus. Pollen grains of Mustard, Cycas & Mango are not winged shaped.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which one is wrongly matched?
questions.explanation: Sol. Polysiphonia is a genus of red algae, where asexual spores and gametes are non- motile or non- flagellated. Other options (1, 2 & 4) are correctly matched.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following options correctly represents the lung conditions in asthma and emphysema, respectively?

options.A: Increased respiratory surface; Inflammation of bronchioles
options.B: Increased number of bronchioles; Increased respiratory surface
options.C: Inflammation of bronchioles; Decreased respiratory surface
options.D: Decreased respiratory surface; Inflammation of bronchioles

questions.explanation: Sol. Asthma is a difficulty in breathing causing wheezing due to inflammation of bronchi and bronchioles. Emphysema is a chronic disorder in which alveolar walls are damaged due to which respiratory surface is decreased.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The transparent lens in the human eye is held in its place by

options.A: smooth muscles attached to the iris
options.C: ligaments attached to the ciliary body
options.D: smooth muscles attached to the ciliary body

questions.explanation: Sol. Lens in the human eye is held in its place by suspensory ligaments attached to the ciliary body.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is an amino acid derived hormone?
questions.explanation: Sol. Epinephrine is derived from tyrosine amino acid by the removal of carboxyl group. It is a catecholamine.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following hormones can play a significant role in osteoporosis?
questions.explanation: Sol. Estrogen promotes the activity of osteoblast and inhibits osteoclast. In an ageing female osteoporosis occurs due to deficiency of estrogen. Parathormone promotes mobilisation of calcium from bone into blood. Excessive activity of parathormone causes demineralisation leading to osteoporosis.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following structures or regions is incorrectly paired with its functions?

options.A: Hypothalamus: production of releasing hormones and regulation of temperature, hunger and thirst.
options.B: Limbic system: consists of fibre tracts that interconnect different regions of brain; controls movement.
options.C: Medulla oblongata: controls respiration and cardiovascular reflexes.
options.D: Corpus callosum: band of fibers connecting left and right cerebral hemispheres.

questions.explanation: Sol. Limbic system is emotional brain. It controls all emotions in our body but not movements.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The amnion of mammalian embryo is derived from
questions.explanation: Sol. The extraembryonic or foetal membranes are amnion, chorion, allantois and Yolk sac. Amnion is formed from mesoderm on outer side and ectoderm on inner side. Chorion is formed from trophoectoderm and mesoderm whereas allantois and Yolk sac membrane have mesoderm on outerside and endoderm in inner side.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Hormones secreted by the placenta to maintain pregnancy are
questions.explanation: Sol. Placenta releases human chorionic gonadotropic hormone (hCG) which stimulates the Corpus luteum during pregnancy to release estrogen and progesterone and also rescues corpus luteum from regression. Human placental lactogen (hPL) is involved in growth of body of mother and breast. Progesterone maintains pregnancy, keeps the uterus silent by increasing uterine threshold to contractile stimuli.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The difference between spermiogenesis and spermatism is

options.A: In spermiogenesis spermatozoa from sertoli cells are released into the cavity of seminiferous tubules, while in spermatism spermatozoa are formed.
options.B: In spermiogenesis spermatozoa are formed, while in spermatism spermatids are formed.
options.C: In spermiogenesis spermatids are formed, while in spermatism spermatozoa are formed.
options.D: In spermiogenesis spermatozoa are formed, while in spermatism spermatozoa are released from sertoli cells into the cavity of seminiferous tubules.

questions.explanation: Explanation not provided in the PDF.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The contraceptive 'SAHELI'

options.B: increases the concentration of estrogen and prevents ovulation in females.
options.C: blocks estrogen receptors in the uterus, preventing eggs from getting implanted.

questions.explanation: Sol. Saheli is the first non- steroidal, once a week pill. It contains centchroman and its functioning is based upon selective Estrogen Receptor modulation.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Ciliates differ from all other protozoans in

options.B: having a contractile vacuole for removing excess water

questions.explanation: Sol. Ciliates differs from other protozoans in having two types of nuclei. eg. Paramoecium have two types of nuclei i.e. macronucleus & micronucleus.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Identify the vertebrate group of animals characterized by crop and gizzard in its digestive system
questions.explanation: Sol. The digestive tract of Aves has additional chambers in their digestive system as crop and Gizzard. Crop is concerned with storage of food grains. Gizzard is a masticatory organ in birds used to crush food grain.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following features is used to identify a male cockroach from a female cockroach?

options.C: Presence of a boat shaped sternum on the 9th abdominal segment

questions.explanation: Sol. Males bear a pair of short, thread like anal styles which are absent in females. Anal/caudal styles arise from 9th abdominal segment in male cockroach.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which one of these animals is not a homeotherm?
questions.explanation: Sol. Homeotherm are animals that maintain constant body temperature, irrespective of surrounding temperature. Birds and mammals are homeotherm. Chelone (Turtle) belongs to class reptilia which is Poikilotherm or cold blood.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following animals does not undergo metamorphosis?
questions.explanation: Sol. Metamorphosis refers to transformation of larva into adult. Animals that perform metamorphosis are said to have indirect development. In earthworm development is direct which means no larval stage and hence no metamorphosis.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following organisms are known as chief producers in the oceans?
questions.explanation: Sol. Diatoms are chief producers of the ocean.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which one of the following population interactions is widely used in medical science for the production of antibiotics?
questions.explanation: Sol. Amensalism/Antibiosis (0, - ) Antibiotics are chemicals secreted by one microbial group (eg: Penicillium) which harm other microbes (eg: Staphyloccus) It has no effect on Penicillium or the organism which produces it.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: All of the following are included in 'ex-situ conservation' except
questions.explanation: Sol. Sacred groves - in-situ conservation. Represent pristine forest patch as protected by Tribal groups.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In a growing population of a country,

options.A: reproductive and pre-reproductive individuals are equal in number.
options.B: reproductive individuals are less than the post-reproductive individuals.
options.C: pre-reproductive individuals are more than the reproductive individuals.
options.D: pre-reproductive individuals are less than the reproductive individuals.

questions.explanation: Sol. Whenever the pre- reproductive individuals or the younger population size is larger than the reproductive group, the population will be an increasing population.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which part of poppy plant is used to obtain the drug "Smack"?
questions.explanation: Sol. 'Smack' also called as brown sugar/Heroin is formed by acetylation of morphine. It is obtained from the latex of unripe capsule of Poppy plant.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: All of the following are part of an operon except
questions.explanation: Sol. Enhancer sequences are present in eukaryotes. Operon concept is for prokaryotes.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: A woman has an X-linked condition on one of her X chromosomes. This chromosome can be inherited by
questions.explanation: Sol. Woman is a carrier. Both son & daughter inherit X-chromosome. Although only son will be the diseased.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: According to Hugo de Vries, the mechanism of evolution is
questions.explanation: Sol. As per mutation theory given by Hugo de Vries, the evolution is a discontinuous phenomenon or saltatory phenomenon/saltation.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: AGGTATCGCAT is a sequence from the coding strand of a gene. What will be the corresponding sequence of the transcribed mRNA?
questions.explanation: Sol. Coding strand and mRNA has same nucleotide sequence except, 'T'- Thymine is replaced by 'U'-Uracil in mRNA.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following gastric cells indirectly help in erythropoiesis?
questions.explanation: Sol. Parietal or oxyntic cell is a source of HCl and intrinsic factor. HCl converts iron present in diet from ferric to ferrous form so that it can be absorbed easily and used during erythropoiesis. Intrinsic factor is essential for the absorption of vitamin B12 and its deficiency causes pernicious anaemia.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is an occupational respiratory disorder?
questions.explanation: Sol. Silicosis is due to excess inhalation of silica dust in the workers involved grinding or stone breaking industries. Long exposure can give rise to inflammation leading to fibrosis and thus causing serious lung damage. Anthrax is a serious infectious disease caused by Bacillus anthracis. It commonly affects domestic and wild animals. Emphysema is a chronic disorder in which alveolar walls are damaged due to which respiratory surface is decreased. Botulism is a form of food poisoning caused by Clostridium botulinum.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Calcium is important in skeletal muscle contraction because it

options.A: Detaches the myosin head from the actin filament.
options.B: Activates the myosin ATPase by binding to it.
options.C: Binds to troponin to remove the masking of active sites on actin for myosin.
options.D: Prevents the formation of bonds between the myosin cross bridges and the actin filament.

questions.explanation: Sol. Signal for contraction increase Ca++ level many folds in the sarcoplasm. Ca++ now binds with sub- unit of troponin (troponin "C") which is masking the active site on actin filament and displaces the sub- unit of troponin. Once the active site is exposed, head of the myosin attaches and initiate contraction by sliding the actin over myosin.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Nissl bodies are mainly composed of
questions.explanation: Sol. Nissl granules are present in the cyton and even extend into the dendrite but absent in axon and rest of the neuron. Nissl granules are in fact composed of free ribosomes and RER. They are responsible for protein synthesis.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of these statements is incorrect?

options.A: Glycolysis operates as long as it is supplied with NAD that can pick up hydrogen atoms
options.C: Enzymes of TCA cycle are present in mitochondrial matrix
options.D: Oxidative phosphorylation takes place in outer mitochondrial membrane

questions.explanation: Sol. Oxidative phosphorylation takes place in inner mitochondrial membrane.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Select the incorrect match:
questions.explanation: Sol. Polytene chromosomes are found in salivary glands of insects of order Diptera.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following terms describe human dention?
questions.explanation: Sol. In humans, dention is Thecodont: Teeth are present in the sockets of the jaw bone called alveoli. Diphyodont: Teeth erupts twice, temporary milk or deciduous teeth are replaced by a set of permanent or adult teeth. Heterodont dention: Dention consists of different types of teeth namely incisors, canine, premolars and molars.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following events does not occur in rough endoplasmic reticulum?
questions.explanation: Sol. Phospholipid synthesis does not take place in RER. Smooth endoplasmic reticulum are involved in lipid synthesis.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Many ribosomes may associate with a single mRNA to form multiple copies of a polypeptide simultaneously. Such strings of ribosomes are termed as
questions.explanation: Sol. The phenomenon of association of many ribosomes with single m- RNA leads to formation of polyribosomes or polysomes or ergasomes.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In which disease does mosquito transmitted pathogen cause chronic inflammation of lymphatic vessels?
questions.explanation: Sol. Elephantiasis is caused by roundworm, Wucheria bancrofti and it is transmitted by Culex mosquito.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Among the following sets of examples for divergent evolution, select the incorrect option:

options.A: Brain of bat, man and cheetah
options.B: Heart of bat, man and cheetah
options.C: Forelimbs of man, bat and cheetah
options.D: Eye of octopus, bat and man

questions.explanation: Sol. Divergent evolution occurs in the same structure, example - forelimbs, heart, brain of vertebrates which have developed along different directions due to adaptation to different needs whereas eye of octopus, bat and man are examples of analogous organs showing convergent evolution.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Conversion of milk to curd improves its nutritional value by increasing the amount of
questions.explanation: Sol. Curd is more nourishing than milk. It has enriched presence of vitamins specially Vit- B12.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The similarity of bone structure in the forelimbs of many vertebrates is an example of
questions.explanation: Sol. In different vertebrates, bones of forelimbs are similar but their forelimbs are adapted in different way as per their adaptation, hence example of homology.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following characteristics represent 'Inheritance of blood groups' in humans? a. Dominance b. Co-dominance c. Multiple alle d. Incomplete dominance e. Polygenic inheritance
questions.explanation: Sol. IA, IB, IO - Dominant- recessive relationship - Codominance - 3- different allelic forms of a gene (multiple allelism)

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following approaches does not give the defined action of contraceptive ?

options.B: Intra uterine devices - Increase phagocytosis of sperms, suppress sperm motility and fertilizing capacity of sperms
options.C: Hormonal contraceptives - Prevent/retard entry of sperms, prevent ovulation and fertilization

questions.explanation: This is a direct fact from NCERT syllabus.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which type of tissue correctly matches with its location ? Tissue Location
questions.explanation: This is a direct fact from NCERT syllabus.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is wrongly matched in the given table ?

options.A: Trichoderma polysporum Cyclosporin A immunosuppressive drug
options.B: Monascus pureus Statins lowering of blood cholesterol
options.C: Streptoccus Streptokinase removal of clot from blood vessel
options.D: Clostridium butylicum Lipase removal of oil stains

questions.explanation: This is a direct fact from NCERT syllabus.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is a reducing sugar?
questions.explanation: A reducing sugar is one that has a free aldehyde group or a free hemiacetal group. Maltose is a disaccharide with a free hemiacetal unit on one of its glucose rings, so it exhibits reducing properties.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Glucose reacts with HNO3 to form
questions.explanation: Nitric acid is a strong oxidizing agent. When glucose is oxidized with concentrated nitric acid, both the aldehyde group and the terminal alcohol group are oxidized to carboxylic acid groups, yielding saccharic acid (glucaric acid).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Carbohydrate is a condensation polymer of
questions.explanation: Carbohydrates are polymers formed by the condensation (joing with the loss of water) of simple sugar monomers (monosaccharides) like glucose and fructose to form disaccharides and polysaccharides.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: On reaction with red P + HI, glucose forms
questions.explanation: Red phosphorus and hydroiodic acid (HI) are strong reducing agents. This reaction reduces the oxygen-containg functional groups of glucose, converting the entire six-carbon chain into n-hexane.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is not a result of cell division?
questions.explanation: Metabolism refers to all the chemical reactions occurring in an organism. It is a cellular process, but not a direct result of cell division. Growth, repair, and reproduction (in unicellular organisms) are direct outcomes of cell division.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Mark the incorrect pair.
questions.explanation: Amoeba reproduces asexually by binary fission, where it splits into two daughter cells. Fragmentation is a process where an organism breaks into pieces, each capable of growing into a new individual, which is not typical for Amoeba.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is incorrect for reproduction?

options.A: Unicellular organisms reproduce by cell division
options.B: Reproduction is a characteristic of all living organisms
options.C: In unicellular organisms, reproduction and growth are linked together
options.D: Non-living objects are incapable of reproducing

questions.explanation: While reproduction is a defing characteristic of life, it is not shown by all living organisms. Examples include sterile worker bees, mules, and infertile human couples who are alive but cannot reproduce.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Mark the incorrect statement w.r.t. metabolism.

options.B: It is the property of all living forms
options.C: The metabolic reactions can be demonstrated in-vitro
options.D: It is not a defing feature of life forms

questions.explanation: Metabolism, the sum total of all chemical reactions occurring in an organism, is a defing feature of all living forms. It is a characteristic that distinguishes living from non-living.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Non-living objects exhibit/show
questions.explanation: Growth in non-living objects, like a sand mound, occurs by the accumulation of material on the surface. This growth is extrinsic and can be reversed by erosion, which is not possible for living growth.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which statement is false about the growth shown by non-living objects?

options.C: The growth is due to the accumulation of material on the surface

questions.explanation: The growth exhibited by non-living objects is extrinsic, meaning it occurs due to the addition of material from the outside. Living growth is intrinsic, occurring from within due to cell division.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Local names of various plants and animals
questions.explanation: Local or common names for organisms vary from region to region and language to language. This variability is why a universal system of binomial nomenclature is essential for scientific communication.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is incorrect w.r.t. Binomial nomenclature?

options.A: Biological names are generally in Latin
options.B: The first word in a biological name represents the genus
options.C: Biological names are printed in italics
options.D: The first word of the genus starts with a small letter

questions.explanation: According to the rules of binomial nomenclature, the first word, which denotes the genus, always starts with a capital letter. The second word, the specific epithet, starts with a small letter.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: What do A, B and C represent in the given scientific name respectively? Mangifera indica Linn C B A

options.A: Generic name, specific name and author’s name
options.B: Specific name, generic name and author’s name
options.C: Author’s name, specific name and generic name
options.D: Generic name, author’s name and specific name

questions.explanation: In the given name, 'Mangifera' is the genus, 'indica' is the specific epithet (species name), and 'Linn' is the abbreviated name of the author (Carolus Linnaeus) who first described the species.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is incorrect regarding scientific names?

options.A: These are also known as common names
options.B: These ensure that each organism has only one name
options.C: These have two components – the generic name and specific epithet

questions.explanation: Scientific names are distinct from common names. They are universally accepted names governed by a set of rules, designed to ensure that every organism has a single, unique, and stable name.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: According to binomial nomenclature, every living organism has

options.A: Two scientific names with single component
options.B: One scientific name with two components
options.C: Two names, one Latin and other common
options.D: One common name with three components
