questions.explanation: Saccharomyces cerevisiae (brewer's yeast) is correctly matched with ethanol production through fermentation. Acetobacter aceti produces acetic acid; Penicillium notatum produces penicillin; Methanobacterium produces methane.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: What gases are produced in anaerobic sludge digesters?
questions.explanation: In anaerobic sludge digesters, methanogenic bacteria produce a mixture of gases including methane (CH₄), carbon dioxide (CO₂), and traces of hydrogen sulphide (H₂S).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Besides paddy fields, cyanobacteria are also found inside vegetative part of
questions.explanation: Cyanobacteria (e.g., Nostoc, Anabaena) are found in the coralloid roots of Cycas (a gymnosperm) where they fix atmospheric nitrogen in a symbiotic association.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: A good producer of citric acid is
questions.explanation: Aspergillus niger is a fungus that is commercially used for the production of citric acid on a large scale.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: During sewage treatment, biogases are produced which include:
questions.explanation: Biogas produced during sewage treatment primarily consists of methane (CH₄), hydrogen sulphide (H₂S), and carbon dioxide (CO₂).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Monascus pureus is a yeast used commercially in the production of

options.C: Streptokinase for removing clots from the blood vessels

questions.explanation: Monascus pureus is a yeast commercially used for the production of statins, which are blood-cholesterol-lowering agents.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In Gobar gas, the maximum amount is that of
questions.explanation: Gobar gas (biogas) consists primarily of methane (50-70%), which is its main combustible component.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Yeast is used in the production of
questions.explanation: Saccharomyces cerevisiae (yeast) is used in the production of bread (through fermentation producing CO₂) and beer (through fermentation producing alcohol).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: A nitrogen fixing microbe associated with Azolla in rice fields is
questions.explanation: The water fern Azolla has a symbiotic association with the nitrogen-fixing cyanobacterium Anabaena azollae, which is used as a biofertilizer in rice paddies.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Measuring Biochemical Oxygen Demand (BOD) is a method used for

options.A: Measuring the activity of Saccharomyces cervisae in producing curd on a commercial scale
options.B: Working out the efficiency of R.B.Cs. about their capacity to carry oxygen
options.C: Estimating the amount of organic matter in sewage water
options.D: Working out the efficiency of oil driven automobile engines

questions.explanation: BOD is a measure of the amount of dissolved oxygen consumed by microorganisms for the oxidation of organic matter in a water sample. It is used to estimate the level of organic pollution in sewage and other water bodies.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The most abundant prokaryotes helpful to humans in making curd from milk and in production of antibiotics are the ones categorised as
questions.explanation: Heterotrophic bacteria are the most abundant prokaryotes. Lactic acid bacteria (used in curd-making) and antibiotic-producing bacteria (like Streptomyces) are heterotrophic.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which one of the following is a wrong matching of a microbe and its industrial product, while the remaing three are correct?
questions.explanation: Clostridium butylicum produces butyric acid, not lactic acid. Lactic acid is produced by Lactobacillus species.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which one of the following is an example of carrying out biological control of pests/diseases using microbes?

options.B: Lady bird beetle against aphids in mustard
options.C: Trichoderma sp. against certain plant pathogens
options.D: Nucleopolyhedrovirus agains white rust in Brassica

questions.explanation: Trichoderma species are free-living fungi that act as biocontrol agents against various soil-borne plant pathogens. Bt-cotton is a genetically engineered plant, ladybird beetle is an insect predator, and NPV is used against insects, not white rust (a fungal disease).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which one of the following microbes forms symbiotic association with plants and helps them in their nutrition?
questions.explanation: Glomus is a genus of arbuscular mycorrhizal fungi that forms symbiotic associations with plant roots, enhancing nutrient (especially phosphorus) and water absorption.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: A population is

options.A: A group of organisms of one species occupying a defined area
options.B: A group of organisms of different species occupying a defined area
options.C: A group of organisms of different species occupying different geographical area
options.D: A group of sexually isolated organisms occupying a defined area

questions.explanation: In ecology, a population is defined as a group of individuals belonging to the same species, living in a defined geographical area at a given time, and capable of interbreeding.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Permafrost condition is characteristic feature of
questions.explanation: Permafrost, a permanently frozen layer of subsoil, is a characteristic feature of cold desert biomes (tundra and polar regions).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The organisms found in boiling thermal springs are
questions.explanation: Archaebacteria, specifically thermoacidophiles, are able to survive in extreme environments like boiling thermal springs due to their unique cell wall and membrane composition.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Mango, tuna fish, snow leopards are
questions.explanation: Stenothermal organisms can tolerate only a narrow range of temperatures. Mango, tuna fish, and snow leopards are restricted to specific temperature zones and are therefore stenothermal.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Smaller animals tend to lose body heat very fast as compared to larger animals because they have

options.C: Equal values of surface and volume

questions.explanation: Smaller animals have a higher surface area to volume ratio, which means they lose body heat more rapidly to the environment compared to larger animals.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Thick cuticle, sunken and scotoactive stomata, CAM photosynthesis and conversion of leaves into spines are some of the important characters of

options.D: More than one option is correct

questions.explanation: These are all xerophytic adaptations to conserve water in dry environments, and are characteristic of both xerophytes and desert plants.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Warm blooded animals like mammals from colder climates generally have shorter ear and limbs. This is an explanation of
questions.explanation: Allen's rule states that animals adapted to cold climates have shorter extremities (ears, limbs, tails) to minimize heat loss by reducing surface area.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In nature, a given habitat has enough resources to support a maximum possible number, beyond which no further growth is possible. This characteristic feature of nature is known as
questions.explanation: Carrying capacity (K) is the maximum population size of a species that an environment can sustain indefinitely without degrading the environment.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Predators play important role in

options.A: Conduction of energy across trophic levels
options.D: More than one option is correct

questions.explanation: Predators play multiple important roles: they act as conduits for energy transfer, help control prey populations, and maintain species diversity by preventing competive exclusion.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: 'Resource partioning' is an important mechanism which promotes
questions.explanation: Resource partioning involves the division of limited resources by species to reduce competion, allowing them to coexist in the same habitat (e.g., different warbler species foraging in different parts of the same tree).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: A J-shaped growth curve depicts

options.A: Exponential growth when conditions are limited
options.B: Exponential growth when conditions are unlimited
options.C: Logistic growth when conditions are limited
options.D: Logistic growth when conditions are unlimited

questions.explanation: A J-shaped curve represents exponential growth, which occurs when resources are unlimited. The population grows rapidly without any checks, followed by a sudden crash.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Mycorrhizae are the example of
questions.explanation: Mycorrhizae represent a mutualistic (+, +) symbiotic association between fungal hyphae and the roots of higher plants, benefiting both partners.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Asymptote in a logistic growth curve is obtained when
questions.explanation: In the logistic growth equation (dN/dt = rN (K-N)/K), when the population size (N) reaches the carrying capacity (K), (K-N)/K becomes zero, so dN/dt = 0, and the curve reaches an asymptote.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Plants which produce characteristic pneumatophores and show vipary belong to
questions.explanation: Halophytes (mangroves) growing in saline, marshy areas show vipary (seed germination while still attached to the parent plant) and produce pneumatophores (breathing roots) for gaseous exchange.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is correct for r-selected species?

options.A: Large number of progeny with small size
options.B: Large number of progeny with large size
options.C: Small number of progeny with small size
options.D: Small number of progeny with large size

questions.explanation: r-selected species are characterized by high reproductive rates (large number of offspring), small body size, little parental care, and populations that fluctuate around the carrying capacity.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: If '+' sign is assigned to beneficial interaction, '–' sign to detrimental and '0' sign to neutral interaction, then the population interaction represented by '+' '–' refers to
questions.explanation: In parasitism (+, –), one organism (the parasite) benefits at the expense of the other (the host). Mutualism (+, +), amensalism (–, 0), commensalism (+, 0).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is correctly matched?
questions.explanation: Parthenium hysterophorus (carrot grass) is an invasive alien species that poses a serious threat to native biodiversity. Aerenchyma is found in hydrophytes, age pyramid is a population characteristic, stratification is a community characteristic.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The following graph depicts changes in two populations (A and B) of herbivores in a grassy field. A possible reason for these changes is that

options.A: Population A consumed the members of population B
options.B: Both plant populations in this habitat decreased
options.C: Population B competed more successfully for food than population A
options.D: Population A produced more offspring than population B

questions.explanation: The graph shows one population increasing while the other decreases, indicating competive exclusion where one species (B) outcompetes the other (A) for limited resources.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Most animals are tree dwellers in a
questions.explanation: Tropical rainforests have a complex vertical structure (stratification) with multiple canopy layers, providing diverse niches and supporting a large number of arboreal (tree-dwelling) animals.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: A sedentary sea anemone gets attached to the shell ling of hermit crab. The association is
questions.explanation: The relationship between sea anemone and hermit crab is symbiotic (mutualistic). The sea anemone gets transported and food scraps, while the crab gets protection from predators due to the anemone's sting cells.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: A biologist studied the population of rats in a barn. He found that the average natality was 250, average mortality 240, immigration 20 and emigration 30. The net increase in population is
questions.explanation: Net increase = (Natality + Immigration) - (Mortality + Emigration) = (250 + 20) - (240 + 30) = 270 - 270 = 0.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Cuscuta is an example of
questions.explanation: Cuscuta (dodder) is an ectoparasitic plant that derives its nutrition by attaching to and twing around the host plant's stem, without entering the host's body internally.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: People who have migrated from the planes to an area adjoing Rohtang pass about six months back

options.A: Have the usual RBC count but their haemoglobin has very high binding affinity to O₂
options.B: Have more RBCs and their haemoglobin has a lower binding affinity of O₂
options.C: Are not physically fit to play games like football
options.D: Suffer from altitude sickness with symptoms like nausea, fatigue, etc.

questions.explanation: At high altitudes, low oxygen availability stimulates increased RBC production (polycythemia) and hemoglobin with lower O₂ binding affinity to facilitate oxygen release to tissues.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The logistic population growth is expressed by the equation
questions.explanation: The correct logistic growth equation is dN/dt = rN (K-N/K), where r is the intrinsic rate of increase, N is population size, and K is carrying capacity.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Large Woody Vines are more commonly found in:
questions.explanation: Large woody vines called lianas are characteristic of tropical rainforests, where they climb trees to reach sunlight in the dense canopy.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which one of the following is categorised as a parasite in true sense?

options.A: The cuckoo (koel) lays its eggs in crow's nest
options.B: The female Anopheles bites and sucks blood from human
options.C: Human foetus developing inside the uterus draws nourishment form the mother
options.D: Head louse living on the human scalp as well as laying eggs on human hair

questions.explanation: Head louse is a true ectoparasite, living on and obtaing nourishment from the host throughout its life cycle. The others are brood parasitism, predation, and gestation respectively.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Consider the following four conditions (a - d) and select the correct pair of them as adaptation to environment in desert lizards. The conditions (a) Burrowing in soil to escape high temperature (b) Losing heat rapidly from the body during high temperature (c) Bask in sun when temperature is low (d) Insulating body due to thick fatty dermis
questions.explanation: Desert lizards exhibit behavioral adaptations: burrowing to escape high temperatures and basking in the sun to raise body temperature when it's cool. They are ectotherms and do not have insulating fat layers.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: What type of human population is represented by the following age pyramid? (Post-reproductive narrow, Reproductive medium, Pre-reproductive broad)
questions.explanation: A broad base (large pre-reproductive population) indicates an expanding or growing population with high birth rates and many young individuals.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The figure given below is a diagrammatic representation of response of organisms to abiotic factors. What do a, b and c represent respectively? (Graph showing internal level vs external level - regulator, conformer, partial regulator)
questions.explanation: Regulators maintain constant internal environment (line horizontal). Conformers change with external environment (diagonal line). Partial regulators show some regulation but eventually conform (curved line).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which one of the following is most appropriately defined?

options.A: Host is an organism which provides food to another organism
options.B: Amensalism is a relationship in which one species is benefited whereas the other is unaffected
options.C: Predator is an organism that catches and kills other organism for food
options.D: Parasite is an organism which always lives inside the body of other organism and may kill it

questions.explanation: A predator is correctly defined as an organism that catches, kills, and consumes another organism (prey). Parasites may live outside (ectoparasites) and don't always kill the host.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which one of the following is one of the characteristics of a biological community?
questions.explanation: Stratification (vertical layering of different species) is a characteristic of a biological community. Sex-ratio, natality, and mortality are population attributes.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Study the four statements (a–d) given below and select the two correct ones out of them (a) A lion eating a deer and a sparrow feeding on grain are ecologically similar in being consumers (b) Predator star fish Pisaster helps in maintaing species diversity of some invertebrates (c) Predators ultimately lead to the extinction of prey species (d) Production of chemicals such as nicotine, strychnine by the plants are metabolic disorders The two correct statements are
questions.explanation: (a) Both are consumers (lion = secondary/tertiary, sparrow = primary). (b) Pisaster is a keystone predator that maintains species diversity by preventing competive exclusion. (c) False - predators rarely cause extinction; they help maintain prey populations. (d) False - these are chemical defenses, not disorders.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: What is true about the isolated small tribal populations?

options.A: There is no change in population size as they have a large gene pool
options.B: There is a decline in population as boys marry girls only from their own tribe
options.C: Hereditary diseases like colour blindness do not spread in the isolated population
options.D: Wrestlers who develop strong body muscles in their life time pass this character on to their progeny

questions.explanation: Isolated small tribal populations often show population decline due to limited gene pool, inbreeding depression, and increased frequency of recessive disorders. Acquired characters (like muscle development) are not inherited.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Quercus species are the dominant component in
questions.explanation: Quercus (oak) species are dominant trees in temperate deciduous forests of the Northern Hemisphere, along with maples, beeches, and birches.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Consider the following four statements (a - d) about certain desert animals such as kangaroo rat. (a) They have dark colour and high rate of reproduction and excrete solid urine. (b) They do not drink water, breathe at a slow rate to conserve water and have their body covered with thick hairs. (c) They feed on dry seeds and do not require drinking water. (d) They excrete very concentrated urine and do not use water to regulate body temperature. Which two of the above statements for such animals are true?
questions.explanation: Kangaroo rats are adapted to deserts: they feed on dry seeds (obtaing metabolic water), excrete highly concentrated urine, and do not use water for temperature regulation (they are nocturnal and burrow).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: If the mean and the median pertaing to a certain character of a population are of the same value, the following is most likely to occur:
questions.explanation: In a normal distribution curve (bell-shaped), the mean, median, and mode are all equal and located at the center of the distribution.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The population of an insect species shows an explosive increase in numbers during rainy season followed by its disappearance at the end of the season. What does this show?

options.A: The population of its predators increases enormously.
options.B: S-shaped or sigmoid growth of this insect.
options.C: The food plants mature and die at the end of the rainy season.
options.D: Its population growth curve is of J-type.

questions.explanation: The pattern shows a J-shaped growth curve: exponential increase during favorable conditions (rainy season) followed by a sudden crash when conditions become unfavorable.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Geometric representation of age structure is a characteristic of
questions.explanation: Age pyramids (geometric representation of age structure) are a characteristic feature used to study and analyze population structure and dynamics.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: A high density of elephant population in an area can result in
questions.explanation: High population density of the same species (elephants) leads to intraspecific competion for limited resources like food, water, and space.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Niche overlap indicates

options.B: Two different parasites on the same host
options.C: Sharing of one or more resources between the two species

questions.explanation: Niche overlap occurs when two species utilize the same limited resources (food, space, etc.), which can lead to competion unless resource partioning occurs.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following represents largest man made ecosystem?
questions.explanation: Agroecosystems (agricultural ecosystems) are the largest man-made ecosystems, covering vast areas of land for crop cultivation and livestock rearing.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: To make the study of global ecosystem easier, it is broadly divided into how many basic categories?
questions.explanation: Ecosystems are broadly divided into two basic categories: terrestrial ecosystems (land-based) and aquatic ecosystems (water-based).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: What percentages of PAR can be captured by plants?
questions.explanation: Plants capture only 2-10% of the Photosynthetically Active Radiation (PAR) for photosynthesis. The rest is reflected, transmitted, or converted to heat.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Rate of production of organic matter by producers per unit time and area is

options.D: More than one option is correct

questions.explanation: Gross Primary Productivity (GPP) is the rate of production of organic matter by producers (plants) through photosynthesis per unit time and area.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Build up of organic material in soil is under which of the following conditions to
questions.explanation: Organic material accumulates in soil under low temperatures (<10°C) and anaerobic conditions, where decomposition rates are slow. Humus formation is favored in such conditions.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Humification results in the formation of ___ substance
questions.explanation: Humification results in the formation of a dark-colored, acidic, amorphous substance called humus, which is highly resistant to microbial action.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Trees → Birds → Lice → Bacteria. Thus food chain is
questions.explanation: This represents a parasitic food chain, starting with a tree (producer), followed by birds (primary consumers), lice (ectoparasites on birds), and bacteria (decomposers/parasites).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: If 10,000 J of energy fall on piece of land, then what is energy available at T₂ level?
questions.explanation: Only 1% of incident solar energy is captured by producers (GPP) = 100 J. At each trophic level, only 10% is transferred. So T₁ (producers) = 100 J, T₂ (primary consumers) = 10 J.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Choose odd one out w.r.t. functional aspects of ecosystem.
questions.explanation: Species composition is a structural aspect of an ecosystem, while energy flow, decomposition, and nutrient cycling are functional aspects.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Major conduit of energy in land and aquatic ecosystem is transferred through ___ and ___ respectively.
questions.explanation: In terrestrial ecosystems, the major energy conduit is the Detritus Food Chain (DFC). In aquatic ecosystems, the Grazing Food Chain (GFC) is the major conduit.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Productivity contributed by oceans is ___ but area covered is comparatively __.
questions.explanation: Oceans cover about 71% of the Earth's surface but contribute only about 55 billion tons of net primary productivity annually (low productivity per unit area).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Mineralisation is performed by
questions.explanation: Mineralisation, the release of inorganic nutrients from organic matter, is performed by saprophytic bacteria and fungi during decomposition.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Major site of decomposition in the soil and water bodies respectively is
questions.explanation: Decomposition in soil occurs mainly in the upper layer (where organic matter accumulates). In water bodies, decomposition occurs mainly in the bottom layer (sediments).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Raw material for decomposition represented by dead organic matter is
questions.explanation: Detritus is dead particulate organic matter (dead plants, animals, their remains, and fecal matter) that serves as the raw material for decomposition.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Amount of inorganic substances present in an ecosystem is ___. Energy flow is a ___ feature of ecosystem.
questions.explanation: Standing state refers to the amount of inorganic nutrients present in an ecosystem. Energy flow is a functional feature, while standing crop (biomass) is a structural feature.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Choose incorrect option w.r.t. amount of living material present in different trophic levels at a given time

options.C: Is always represented as dry weight only
options.D: Expressed both as biomass and number

questions.explanation: Standing crop can be expressed as biomass (dry weight), as number of individuals, or as energy content. It is not always represented as dry weight only.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The most common shape of pyramid of number is
questions.explanation: The pyramid of numbers is typically upright in most ecosystems, with producers (plants) being most numerous, followed by decreasing numbers at higher trophic levels.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following trophic levels are occupied by sparrow?
questions.explanation: Sparrows can occupy multiple trophic levels: as primary consumers when feeding on seeds/grains, and as secondary consumers when feeding on insects.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following organisms were not given any place in ecological pyramids?

options.D: More than one option is correct

questions.explanation: Decomposers and parasites are not included in ecological pyramids as they do not fit neatly into a single trophic level and operate across multiple levels.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following food chain shows spindle shaped pyramid of number?
questions.explanation: A spindle-shaped pyramid of numbers occurs when a single tree (producer) supports many herbivorous birds, which are then fed upon by fewer hawks.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In hydrarch succession, reed swamp stage is preced by
questions.explanation: Hydrosere succession sequence: Phytoplankton → Submerged plants → Floating plants → Reed swamp → Sedge meadow → Scrub → Forest.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Ecological succession is ___ and ___ change in species composition.
questions.explanation: Ecological succession is an orderly, sequential, and predictable process of change in the species composition of a community over time.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Pioneer community established on a bare rock is
questions.explanation: Lichens are the pioneer species in xerarch (primary) succession on bare rocks. They secrete acids that dissolve rocks, contributing to weathering and soil formation.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Succession proceeds from

options.D: More than one option is correct

questions.explanation: All ecological successions, whether starting from xeric (dry) or hydric (wet) conditions, ultimately progress toward mesic (moderate) conditions, leading to a climax community.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Choose the correct change during ecological succession.
questions.explanation: During succession, decomposers (bacteria and fungi) change along with the plant and animal communities at different seral stages. Total biomass and diversity increase, while humus content increases.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Initial seral stages in succession
questions.explanation: Early seral stages are characterized by low species diversity, simple food chains, and low niche specialization. Diversity increases as succession progresses toward the climax.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Area where succession can take several thousands years to reach climax community is
questions.explanation: Sand dunes represent primary succession on bare, nutrient-poor substrate with harsh conditions, making succession extremely slow, taking thousands of years to reach climax.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Choose the correct sequence of transitional communities in Xerarch succession.
questions.explanation: Xerarch succession on rocks: Lichens (crustose → foliose → fruticose) → Mosses (bryophytes) → Herbs → Shrubs → Trees (climax forest).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Carbon constitutes ___ of dry weight of organism and ___ percentage is dissolved in oceans out of total global carbon.
questions.explanation: Carbon constitutes about 49% of the dry weight of organisms. Approximately 71% of the total global carbon is dissolved in oceans as bicarbonate and carbonate ions.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Choose odd one out w.r.t. gaseous cycle.
questions.explanation: Phosphorus cycle is a sedimentary cycle, with its reservoir in the Earth's crust (rocks). Carbon, hydrogen, and nitrogen cycles are gaseous cycles with atmospheric reservoirs.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: According to Robert Constanza average price tag for ecosystem services is
questions.explanation: According to Robert Constanza and colleagues, the average price tag for global ecosystem services is estimated at US $33 trillion per year.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which is not a structural aspect of ecosystem?
questions.explanation: Productivity is a functional aspect of an ecosystem, not a structural one. Species composition, diversity, and life cycles are structural components.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Stratification is well developed in
questions.explanation: Tropical rain forests exhibit well-developed stratification (vertical layering) with multiple distinct layers: emergent, canopy, understory, shrub, and ground layers.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following ecosystems has highest productivity?
questions.explanation: Tropical rain forests have the highest primary productivity among terrestrial ecosystems due to optimal temperature, moisture, and sunlight year-round.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: For net primary productivity energy captured is
questions.explanation: Net primary productivity (NPP) represents about 0.8-4% of the total incident solar radiation, after accounting for reflection, transmission, and respiration losses.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Least productive ecosystem is
questions.explanation: Tundra ecosystems have the lowest primary productivity due to extreme cold, short growing seasons, and limited nutrient availability.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following are called key industry animals?
questions.explanation: Herbivores are called key industry animals because they form the critical link between producers and higher trophic levels, converting plant matter into animal biomass.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Find an incorrect statement for DFC

options.D: Plays major role in terrestrial systems

questions.explanation: Detritus Food Chain (DFC) starts with dead organic matter (detritus), not solar energy. Solar energy is the starting point for Grazing Food Chains (GFC).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In tropical rain forest most of the energy in ecosystem flows through
questions.explanation: In tropical rain forests, most energy flows through the detritus food chain due to rapid decomposition of organic matter in warm, humid conditions.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The respiratory loss of producers, herbivores and carnivores are respectively
questions.explanation: Respiratory losses increase at higher trophic levels: producers lose about 20% of assimilated energy, herbivores about 30%, and carnivores about 60% through respiration.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which is not true for humus?

options.C: Act as reservoir of nutrients and increases water holding capacity of soil
options.D: They are degradation product of protein and fats and are produced by the process of mineralisation

questions.explanation: Humus is formed by decomposition of lignin and cellulose, not primarily proteins and fats. It is produced by humification, not mineralization (which releases inorganic nutrients).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Nutrient immobilisation

options.B: Is incorporation of nutrients in microbes
options.C: Is covalent linking of nutrients with one another
options.D: More than one option is correct

questions.explanation: Nutrient immobilisation is the incorporation of nutrients into microbial biomass, which prevents nutrient leaching by temporarily holding them in organic form.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: A food web

options.A: Increases variety of food at each trophic level
options.B: Delicately balances the inter relations amongst organisms
options.C: Decreases variety of food but increases quantity of food at each trophic level
options.D: Increases variety as well as quantity of food at each trophic level

questions.explanation: Food webs increase both the variety and quantity of food available at each trophic level by providing multiple interconnected food chains and alternative food sources.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Grazing food chain

options.A: Provides inorganic matter to detritus food chain
options.B: Binds the inorganic nutrients into organic matter
options.C: Is based on energy present in detritus
options.D: Does not add energy into ecosystem

questions.explanation: Grazing food chains begin with living plants that bind inorganic nutrients into organic matter through photosynthesis, making energy available to consumers.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following statement is not correct about energy flow in an ecosystem?

options.A: Pyramid of energy is always straight

questions.explanation: Energy flow in ecosystems is unidirectional, not cyclic. It enters as sunlight, flows through trophic levels, and is lost as heat. Only matter cycles.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Pyramid of number in a cropland ecosystem is
questions.explanation: In cropland ecosystems, the pyramid of numbers is upright, with many producers (crop plants), fewer primary consumers (herbivores), and even fewer secondary consumers.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following ecological pyramids is most representative of functional characteristics of an ecosystem?
questions.explanation: The pyramid of energy is most representative of functional characteristics as it shows the rate of energy flow and productivity at each trophic level, and is always upright.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: If producer is a large tree that supports a number of herbivorous animals which are further attacked by ectoparasites, the pyramid of number shall be
questions.explanation: This creates an inverted pyramid of numbers: one tree (producer) supports many herbivores (primary consumers), which support even more ectoparasites (secondary consumers).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Energy requirement for maintenance of body ___ with successively higher trophic level.
questions.explanation: Energy requirements for maintenance increase at higher trophic levels due to greater activity, more complex homeostasis, and higher metabolic rates of consumers.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: What does the given ecological pyramid represent? (PC 21 kg m⁻², PP 4 kg m⁻²)

options.A: Inverted pyramid of number in pond
options.C: Inverted pyramid of biomass in pond
options.D: Pyramid of biomass in a forest ecosystem

questions.explanation: In aquatic ecosystems like ponds, the biomass pyramid is often inverted because phytoplankton (producers) have high turnover but low standing biomass compared to zooplankton and fish.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Mark the correct match
questions.explanation: Cockroaches and crows are omnivores, feeding on both plant and animal matter. Zooplankton are primary consumers, viruses are not colonisers, and valleys/forests are macroecosystems.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Occur on newly cooled lava, Fater succession, Progress to mesic condition, Occurs on flooded land, Slower succession, changes in flora only. From the above given features, how many are associated with primary succession?
questions.explanation: Primary succession occurs on newly cooled lava, progresses to mesic conditions, and is slower succession. The other features (faster succession, flooded land, changes in flora only) are associated with secondary succession.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Climax communities

options.A: Are more diverse than pioneer communities
options.B: Are less stable than pioneer communities
options.C: Have greater entropy than pioneer communities
options.D: Have a large number but fewer species than pioneer communities

questions.explanation: Climax communities are more stable, more diverse, and have higher species richness than pioneer communities, with complex food webs and niche specialization.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In the hydrosere the reed-swamp stage is followed by
questions.explanation: Hydrosere sequence: Phytoplankton → Submerged plants → Floating plants → Reed swamp → Marsh-meadow → Scrub → Forest.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Consider the following statements about carbon cycle and choose how many statements are correct? (a) Atmosphere has 71% of total global carbon (b) Decomposers contribute substantially to CO₂ pool by their processing of waste materials and dead organic matter of land or oceans (c) 4 × 10¹³ kg carbon is fixed through photo-synthesis annually (d) No respiratory input into atmosphere (e) Carbon constitutes 49% of dry wt. of organisms
questions.explanation: Correct statements: (b) Decomposers release CO₂, (c) About 4 × 10¹³ kg carbon fixed annually, (e) Carbon is 49% of dry weight. (a) Oceans have 71% carbon, not atmosphere; (d) Respiration inputs CO₂ to atmosphere.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Atmospheric input is negligible in
questions.explanation: The phosphorus cycle is sedimentary, with the main reservoir in rocks and minerals. Atmospheric input of phosphorus is negligible compared to gaseous cycles like carbon, nitrogen, and oxygen.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The phosphates remain outside the natural cycle for a long time

options.A: When they form compounds with metals
options.B: When they are incorporated in bone and teeth
options.C: When the bodies of the organisms excrete and decompose

questions.explanation: Phosphates can be locked out of the cycle for long periods when they form insoluble compounds with metals (like calcium phosphate) or when incorporated into bones and teeth of animals.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which ecosystem has the maximum biomass?
questions.explanation: Forest ecosystems have the maximum biomass due to large trees accumulating organic matter over long periods. High productivity ecosystems include tropical rain forests, coral reefs, and estuaries.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Presence of plants arranged into well defined vertical layers depending on their height can be seen best in
questions.explanation: Tropical rain forests exhibit the most distinct vertical stratification with five layers: ground vegetation, shrubs, understory trees, canopy trees, and emergent trees.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The primary producers of the deep-sea hydrothermal vent ecosystem are
questions.explanation: In deep-sea hydrothermal vent ecosystems, chemosynthetic bacteria (archaebacteria) are the primary producers, using chemical energy from hydrogen sulfide to synthesize organic matter.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following taxon shows maximum species diversity?
questions.explanation: Beetles (order Coleoptera) show maximum species diversity among all taxa. It is estimated that one out of every four animal species on Earth is a beetle.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The variation shown by the medicinal plant Rauwolfia vomitoria growing in different Himalayan ranges represents
questions.explanation: Different populations of Rauwolfia vomitoria showing variation in the concentration of reserpine (active chemical) represent genetic diversity within the same species.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: More conservative and scientifically sound estimate made by Robert May places the global species diversity at about
questions.explanation: Robert May's estimate places global species diversity at about 7 million, which is more conservative than earlier estimates of 20-50 million.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Insects are the most numerous with estimate of
questions.explanation: Insects are the most numerous group, accounting for about 7 out of every 10 animal species on Earth.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In global biodiversity of plants, the
questions.explanation: Among plants, angiosperms (flowering plants) represent the maximum number of species, followed by fungi, algae, bryophytes, and pteridophytes.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Conventional taxonomic methods are not suitable for identifying
questions.explanation: Conventional taxonomic methods based on morphology are not suitable for identifying microbial species because many microorganisms cannot be cultured and lack distinct morphological features.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: India has __% of the world's land area, its share of the global species diversity is an impressive __%.
questions.explanation: India has only 2.4% of the world's land area but contributes approximately 8.1% to global species diversity, making it one of the megadiverse countries.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: What is the approximate ratio of animals and plants species in our country?
questions.explanation: India has approximately 45,000 plant species and nearly twice that number (about 90,000) of animal species, giving a ratio of roughly 2:1 (animals:plants).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Select incorrect statement w.r.t. greater biological diversity in tropics (A) Tropical latitudes have remained relatively undisturbed for millions of years and thus had a long evolutionary time for species diversification. (B) Tropical environments are more seasonal and less predictable than temperates. (C) There is more solar energy available which contributes to higher productivity.
questions.explanation: Tropical environments are less seasonal, more constant, and more predictable than temperate environments, not more seasonal. This stability promotes niche specialization and diversity.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Relationship between species richness (S) and area (A) represented in the following graph is described by the equation

options.A: log S = log A + Z log C
options.B: log S = log C + Z log A
options.C: log C = log A + S log A
options.D: log C = log S + Z log A

questions.explanation: The species-area relationship is given by S = CA^Z. Taking logarithms: log S = log C + Z log A, where Z is the slope of the line (regression coefficient).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Species-area relationships among very large areas like the entire continents the Z values in the range of
questions.explanation: For very large areas like entire continents, the slope Z of the species-area relationship ranges from 0.6 to 1.2. For smaller areas, Z ranges from 0.1 to 0.2.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following ecologist gave 'rivet popper' hypothesis?
questions.explanation: Paul Ehrlich proposed the 'rivet popper' hypothesis, comparing species in an ecosystem to rivets in an airplane. Each species loss weakens the ecosystem, like each popped rivet weakens the plane.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Colonisation of tropical pacific Islands by humans have resulted in extinction of more than __ species of native birds.
questions.explanation: Human colonization of tropical Pacific islands led to the extinction of more than 2000 species of native birds, primarily due to habitat destruction, hunting, and introduction of invasive species.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: IUCN Red List (2004) documents the extinction of A species in the last B years.
questions.explanation: The IUCN Red List (2004) documented the extinction of 784 species (including 338 vertebrates, 359 invertebrates, and 87 plants) in the last 500 years.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Loss of biodiversity in a region may lead to all of the following, except

options.C: Increased variability in certain ecosystem processes

questions.explanation: Biodiversity loss never increases endemism. Endemism (species restricted to a particular area) typically decreases with biodiversity loss as species become extinct or their ranges shrink.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Presently A% of all mammal species and B% of all gymnosperm species in the world face threat of extinction.
questions.explanation: Currently, about 23% of all mammal species and 31% of all gymnosperm species face the threat of extinction worldwide.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The Evil Quartet represents the major causes of
questions.explanation: The "Evil Quartet" refers to the four major causes of biodiversity loss: habitat loss and fragmentation, over-exploitation, alien species invasions, and co-extinctions.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is the most important cause driving animals and plants to extinction?
questions.explanation: Habitat loss and fragmentation is the single most important cause driving species to extinction, affecting both plants and animals by destroying their natural environments.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following organisms are badly affected when large habitats are broken up into small fragments due to various human activities?
questions.explanation: Animals with migratory habits are most severely affected by habitat fragmentation, as they require large, contiguous habitats for their migration routes and cannot complete their life cycles in small fragments.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Extinction of more than 200 species of cichlid fish in lake Victoria was due to introduction of
questions.explanation: The introduction of Nile perch (Lates niloticus) into Lake Victoria caused the extinction of more than 200 species of native cichlid fish through predation and competion.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: When a host fish species becomes extinct, its unique assemblage of parasites also meets the same fate. It is an example of
questions.explanation: Co-extinction refers to the extinction of one species (like a parasite) following the extinction of another species (its host) on which it depends obligately.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: More than __% of the drugs currently sold in the market worldwide are derived from plants.
questions.explanation: More than 25% of the drugs currently sold in the global market are derived from plants, highlighting the economic value of biodiversity.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Bio-prospecting is exploring molecular, genetic and species level diversity for products of
questions.explanation: Bio-prospecting is the exploration of biodiversity at molecular, genetic, and species levels for commercially valuable products and economic benefits, such as new drugs, crops, and industrial materials.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The fast dwindling A forest is estimated to produce B percent of the total oxygen in earth's atmosphere.
questions.explanation: The Amazon rainforest, which is rapidly being deforested, is estimated to produce about 20% of the total oxygen in Earth's atmosphere.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: How many biodiversity hot spots have been identified in the world?
questions.explanation: Currently, 34 biodiversity hotspots have been identified worldwide, covering less than 2% of the land surface but containg about 20% of the human population and a high percentage of endemic species.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Strict protection of biodiversity hotspots could reduce ongoing mass extinctions by almost
questions.explanation: Strict protection of biodiversity hotspots could reduce ongoing mass extinctions by almost 30%, as these areas contain a high concentration of endemic species under threat.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: "When we conserve and protect the whole ecosystem, its biodiversity at all levels is protected." This approach includes all of the following, except
questions.explanation: Seed banks are ex-situ conservation methods, not in-situ (on-site) methods. Biosphere reserves, national parks, and sanctuaries are in-situ conservation approaches that protect whole ecosystems.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In which of the following method threatened animals are taken out from natural habitat and placed in special setting?
questions.explanation: Zoological parks are ex-situ conservation facilities where threatened animals are taken out of their natural habitat and placed in protected settings for breeding and care.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Gametes of threatened species can be preserved in viable and fertile condition for long periods using
questions.explanation: Cryopreservation involves preserving gametes, embryos, or seeds at extremely low temperatures (liquid nitrogen, -196°C) to maintain them in viable and fertile condition for long periods.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Select incorrect match
questions.explanation: The Aravalli hills are primarily in Rajasthan, not Gujarat. Khasi and Jaintia hills are correctly matched with Meghalaya.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: How many countries pledged their commitment to achieve by 2010, a significant reduction in the current rate of biodiversity loss at global, regional and local levels in the World Summit held in 2002?
questions.explanation: At the World Summit on Sustainable Development in Johannesburg (2002), 190 countries pledged to achieve a significant reduction in the rate of biodiversity loss by 2010.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which is not true regarding genetic diversity?

options.A: It enables a population to adapt to its environment
options.B: It is also basis of speciation

questions.explanation: Higher genetic diversity increases variation, not uniformity. It enables adaptation, speciation, and ecotype formation by providing the raw material for natural selection.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Regional diversity is also called
questions.explanation: Gamma diversity refers to regional diversity (total species diversity across a large geographic area). Alpha diversity is local diversity, beta diversity measures species turnover between habitats.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Beta diversity

options.C: Is generally calculated by dividing gamma diversity by alpha diversity

questions.explanation: Beta diversity (species turnover between habitats) is often calculated as gamma diversity (regional) divided by alpha diversity (local). It measures the rate of species change across habitats.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: India covers the world land area of ___ and contributes the global diversity of __.
questions.explanation: India covers 2.4% of the world's land area but contributes approximately 8.1% to global species diversity, making it a megadiverse country.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Read the following statements: (A) India has a greater ecosystem diversity than Norway. (B) According to the IUCN (2004), the total number of plant and animal species described so far is slightly more than 15 millions.
questions.explanation: (A) is correct: India has greater ecosystem diversity than Norway due to its tropical location and varied climate. (B) is incorrect: IUCN (2004) reported about 1.5 million species described, not 15 million.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which is not a reason of maximum diversity in tropics?
questions.explanation: Tropical environments show less seasonal variation and are more constant, not more variable. This stability allows for niche specialization and higher diversity.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Select an incorrect statement w.r.t. biodiversity

options.A: Mountain peaks are more diverse than foot hills
options.B: Valleys are more diverse than mountain peaks
options.C: Shade sides are more diverse than sun sides of mountains
options.D: Western ghats are more diverse than eastern ghats

questions.explanation: Mountain peaks have lower biodiversity than foothills due to harsher conditions (lower temperature, less oxygen, thinner soil). Valleys and shaded slopes generally have higher diversity.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Read the following statements: (A) Tropics harbour more species than temperate or polar areas. (B) Colombia located near the equator has nearly 1400 species of birds. (C) India has less than 105 species of birds.
questions.explanation: (C) is incorrect: India has more than 1200 species of birds because most of its land area lies in the tropics. (A) and (B) are correct statements.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Amazonian rain forest in South America is home to more than A species of plants and B species of fishes.
questions.explanation: The Amazon rainforest is home to more than 40,000 species of plants and about 3,000 species of fishes, making it one of the most biodiverse regions on Earth.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following statement is wrong?

options.A: A stable community should show much variation in productivity from year to year
options.B: According to Tillman's long term ecosystem experiments increased diversity contributed to higher productivity
options.C: Stable community must be resistant to occasional disturbances
options.D: Rich biodiversity is essential for ecosystem health but imperative for the very survival of the human race on this planet

questions.explanation: A stable community does NOT show much variation in productivity from year to year. Stability implies consistent productivity and resistance to disturbances.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Degree of biodiversity increases from
questions.explanation: Biodiversity increases from poles to equator (latitudinal gradient). Tropical regions near the equator have the highest biodiversity, which decreases as we move toward higher latitudes.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which sample area shows maximum diversity?
questions.explanation: Maximum diversity occurs when species from taxonomically different groups occur in almost equal abundance. Option A includes birds, insects, and mammals with equal representation (4 each), showing higher taxonomic diversity.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Threats to biodiversity comes from
questions.explanation: Threats to biodiversity include habitat loss and fragmentation, over-exploitation, intensive agriculture, alien species invasions, pollution, and climate change.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: A population characteristic of a species susceptible to extinction is

options.A: Low trophic level in food chain
options.B: Inability to switch over to alternate food source

questions.explanation: Species that cannot switch to alternate food sources (specialists) are more susceptible to extinction when their primary food source becomes scarce, compared to generalists that can adapt.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Number of red list categories prepared by WCU (IUCN) is
questions.explanation: The IUCN Red List has 8 categories: Extinct (EX), Extinct in the Wild (EW), Critically Endangered (CR), Endangered (EN), Vulnerable (VU), Near Threatened (NT), Least Concern (LC), and Data Deficient (DD).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: According to IUCN, when a taxon is facing an extremely high risk of extinction in the immediate future, it is
questions.explanation: Critically Endangered (CR) species face an extremely high risk of extinction in the immediate future. Endangered (EN) face a very high risk, and Vulnerable (VU) face a high risk.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Exotic species Eichhornia crassipes

options.A: Was introduced by Europeans in India
options.C: Was introduced in Lake Victoria of South Africa
options.D: Is a straggling shrub of tropical America

questions.explanation: Eichhornia crassipes (water hyacinth) was introduced in India by Europeans for ornamental purposes. It has since become an invasive weed, causing ecological and economic damage.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Silent valley of Kerala is being preserved because it has
questions.explanation: Silent Valley in Kerala is preserved as a national park because it harbors rare and endemic species of plants and animals, representing one of the last remaing tracts of undisturbed tropical rainforest in India.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In India, forests constitute about
questions.explanation: Forests constitute about 19.4% of India's land area, which is below the recommended 33% for plains and 67% for hills according to the National Forest Policy.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Protected areas are example of
questions.explanation: Protected areas like national parks, wildlife sanctuaries, and biosphere reserves are examples of in-situ (on-site) conservation, protecting species in their natural habitats.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The Indian lion is the most important protected species in
questions.explanation: Gir National Park in Gujarat is the only natural habitat of the Asiatic lion (Panthera leo persica) in India, making it critically important for their conservation.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Government of India has provided the private ownership rights in
questions.explanation: In wildlife sanctuaries, the Government of India has provided for private ownership rights to continue, allowing limited human activities, unlike national parks where no private rights exist.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Wild populations of plants and animals and traditional life styles of tribals are protected in
questions.explanation: Biosphere reserves are designed to protect entire ecosystems, including wildlife, traditional lifestyles of tribal communities, and genetic diversity through a system of core, buffer, and transition zones.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which is not a criteria used for determing hot spots?

options.C: Having traditional strategy for protection of biodiversity

questions.explanation: Biodiversity hotspots are determined by two criteria: (1) high degree of endemism (at least 1,500 vascular plants as endemics) and (2) severe threat (at least 70% of original habitat lost).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is correct match w.r.t. biodiversity rich region?
questions.explanation: India has 448 wildlife sanctuaries (correct match). National parks are about 90, biosphere reserves are 18, and zoological parks are numerous across the country.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Select the odd one w.r.t. ex-situ conservation.
questions.explanation: Sacred groves are examples of in-situ conservation (protected forest fragments with religious significance). Botanical gardens, wildlife safari parks, and zoological parks are ex-situ conservation methods.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Mark the odd one out (w.r.t. National Parks)

options.A: Cultivation of land is not permitted
options.B: Meant for protection of both flora and fauna

questions.explanation: Grazing is not allowed in national parks as they are strictly protected areas with no human interference permitted. In sanctuaries, limited grazing may be allowed.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Match the column-I with column-II Column-I | Column-II a. Artemesia annua | i. Henna b. Lawsonia inermis | ii. Anticancer drug c. Vinca rosea | iii. Kattha d. Acacia catechu | iv. Antimalarial drug
questions.explanation: Correct matches: Artemesia annua produces artemisin (antimalarial drug); Lawsonia inermis produces henna; Vinca rosea produces vincristine/vinblastine (anticancer drugs); Acacia catechu produces kattha.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following statement for biosphere reserve is incorrect?

options.A: They are meant for preserving genetic diversity in representative ecosystems
options.B: They are also notified as National Parks
options.C: Transition zone is legally protected undisturbed ecosystem
options.D: Importance in conservation, development, scientific research and monitoring

questions.explanation: In biosphere reserves, the transition zone is NOT legally protected and allows human activities like cropping, forestry, and settlements. The core zone is legally protected and undisturbed.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which one of the following is not a Bird Sanctuary?
questions.explanation: Singhori sanctuary is not primarily a bird sanctuary; it's known for other wildlife. Govind Sagar (Himachal), Ranganathittu (Karnataka), and Ghana (Bharatpur, Rajasthan) are important bird sanctuaries.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Compressed Natural Gas (CNG) is
questions.explanation: Compressed Natural Gas (CNG) primarily consists of methane (CH₄). It is used as a cleaner alternative to petrol and diesel to reduce vehicular pollution.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The electrostatic precipitator can remove over __ particulate matter.
questions.explanation: Electrostatic precipitators can remove over 99% of particulate matter from industrial exhaust by giving particles an electrostatic charge and collecting them on oppositely charged plates.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Find incorrect statement w.r.t. catalytic converter

options.B: Lead in petrol activates the catalysts
options.C: Reduced the emission of poisonous gases
options.D: CO and NOx changed to CO₂ and N₂ gas

questions.explanation: Lead in petrol INACTIVATES (poisons) the catalysts in catalytic converters, which is why unleaded petrol is required for vehicles equipped with catalytic converters.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Among the 41 most polluted cities of the world, Delhi ranked
questions.explanation: Among the 41 most polluted cities in the world, Delhi ranked fourth, prompting significant measures like converting public transport to CNG to reduce vehicular pollution.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: By the end of 2002 in Delhi which step is completed for reducing vehicular pollution?

options.C: Buses were converted to run on CNG

questions.explanation: By the end of 2002, all public transport buses in Delhi were converted to run on Compressed Natural Gas (CNG) as a major step to reduce vehicular pollution.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Euro-II is emission norms for reducing
questions.explanation: Euro-II emission norms focus on reducing sulphur and aromatic hydrocarbon content in fuels to lower vehicular emissions of harmful pollutants.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Most hazardous pollutant of automobile exhaust is
questions.explanation: Lead from automobile exhaust is highly hazardous as it interferes with hemoglobin synthesis, oxygen and glucose metabolism, and can cause anemia, vomiting, convulsions, and neurological damage.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The major source of noise pollution, world wide is due to

options.B: Oil refineries and thermal power plants

questions.explanation: The transport system (road traffic, aircraft, trains) is the major source of noise pollution worldwide, with jet planes generating sounds over 150 dB.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Pollutants which can be removed by a scrubber
questions.explanation: Scrubbers are used to remove gaseous pollutants like sulfur dioxide (SO₂) from industrial exhaust by spraying a liquid (usually lime or water) to dissolve or react with the pollutant.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In automobiles catalytic converters change unburnt hydrocarbons into
questions.explanation: Catalytic converters oxidize unburnt hydrocarbons and carbon monoxide to carbon dioxide (CO₂) and water (H₂O), while reducing nitrogen oxides to nitrogen gas.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: World's most problematic aquatic weed is
questions.explanation: Eichhornia crassipes (water hyacinth) is the world's most problematic aquatic weed due to its rapid vegetative propagation through offsets, covering water surfaces and causing ecological and economic damage.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Ramesh Chandra Dagar includes ___ for case study of organic farming.
questions.explanation: Ramesh Chandra Dagar's case study of organic farming in Sonipat (Haryana) integrated multiple components: bee-keeping, dairy management, water harvesting, composting, and cropping without chemicals.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The domestic sewage in large cities

options.A: Has a high BOD as it contains both aerobic and anaerobic bacteria
options.B: Is processed by aerobic and then anaerobic bacteria in the secondary treatment in Sewage Treatment Plants (STPs)
options.C: When treated in STPs does not really require the aeration step as the sewage contains adequate oxygen
options.D: Has very low amounts of suspended solids and dissolved salts

questions.explanation: Domestic sewage in large cities is processed in STPs by aerobic bacteria (aeration tank) followed by anaerobic bacteria (anaerobic sludge digester) in secondary treatment.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Prime contaminants leading to cultural or accelerated eutrophication are
questions.explanation: Phosphates and nitrates from agricultural runoff, sewage, and detergents are the primary contaminants causing cultural eutrophication, leading to algal blooms and oxygen depletion.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Ecosanitation is

options.A: Sustainable system for handling human excreta
options.B: Sustainable system for handling agricultural wastes
options.C: Sustainable system for handling industrial effluents

questions.explanation: Ecological sanitation (ecosanitation) is a sustainable system for handling human excreta using dry composting toilets, recycling nutrients, and preventing water pollution.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In an aquatic food chain, if water body is having 0.003 ppb of DDT, its maximum concentration can be observed in
questions.explanation: Through biomagnification, DDT concentration increases at each trophic level: phytoplankton (0.04 ppm) → small fish (0.5 ppm) → large fish (2 ppm) → fish-eating birds (25 ppm).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Algal bloom in a lake
questions.explanation: Algal blooms increase CO₂ uptake during day but lead to oxygen depletion at night due to respiration. When algae die, decomposition further depletes oxygen, causing fish kills.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Thermal pollution is more prevalent near
questions.explanation: Thermal pollution is most prevalent near coal-based thermal power plants and nuclear reactors, which use large amounts of water for cooling and discharge heated water into water bodies.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In a polluted lake

options.B: BOD is high and DO is low
options.D: BOD is low and DO is high

questions.explanation: In polluted lakes, high organic matter leads to high Biochemical Oxygen Demand (BOD) as microbes consume oxygen, resulting in low Dissolved Oxygen (DO) levels.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Hospital wastes are
questions.explanation: Hospital wastes are hazardous (contain pathogens, sharps, chemicals) and are disposed of by incineration at high temperatures (900-1300°C) to sterilize and reduce volume.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The raw material for making polyblend is
questions.explanation: Polyblend is made from plastic film waste (recycled plastic) mixed with bitumen. It was developed by Ahmed Khan to create more durable roads while addressing plastic waste disposal.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Integrated organic farming has been adopted in India by
questions.explanation: Ramesh Chandra Dagar adopted integrated organic farming in Sonipat, Haryana, combing agriculture, horticulture, dairy, poultry, aquaculture, and biogas generation in a sustainable system.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: During the past century, the temperature of earth has increased by
questions.explanation: During the past century, the Earth's average surface temperature has increased by approximately 0.6°C due to global warming from increased greenhouse gas emissions.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: What percentage of forest for hills has been recommended by National Forest Polices (1988) of India?
questions.explanation: The National Forest Policy (1988) recommends 67% forest cover for hills and mountainous regions, and 33% for plains areas to maintain ecological balance.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Recognizing the deleterious effects of ozone depletion an international treaty known as ___ was signed at ___
questions.explanation: The Montreal Protocol was signed in Montreal, Canada (1987) to phase out ozone-depleting substances like chlorofluorocarbons (CFCs).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Snow blindness is caused by
questions.explanation: Snow blindness (photokeratis) is caused by UV-B radiation reflected from snow, which damages the cornea and conjunctiva, causing temporary vision loss and pain.
